dead-worker's bead is also reclaimed to open\n\n# === Test 6: Multi-workspace claiming ===\n# Create two workspaces, claim --any gets highest priority across both\nmkdir /tmp/ws-a && cd /tmp/ws-a && bf init --prefix wa\nmkdir /tmp/ws-b && cd /tmp/ws-b && bf init --prefix wb\nbf create --title \"ws-a high\" --priority 0   # in ws-b\nbf create --title \"ws-a low\"  --priority 4   # in ws-b\ncd /tmp/ws-a && bf create --title \"ws-a mid\" --priority 2\nbf claim --any --assignee \"multi-worker\" --json --workspace /tmp/ws-a --workspace /tmp/ws-b\n# Should get ws-b's \"ws-a high\" (priority 0)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Order\n\n| Phase | Description | Estimated Effort |\n|-------|-------------|-----------------|\n| 1 | Core library (model, JSONL read/write, config, ID gen) | Foundation |\n| 2 | SQLite cache layer (schema, storage, sync, doctor) | Medium |\n| 3 | CLI commands (all br-compatible commands) | Medium |\n| 4 | Concurrent claiming (BEGIN IMMEDIATE, impact scoring, stale reclamation, multi-workspace) | Medium |\n| 4B | Extended features (log rotation, batch ops, annotations, operation history, secret scanning, critical path, velocity scoring) | Medium |\n| 5 | NEEDLE integration (pluck.sh update, backward compat) | Small |\n| 6 | Build & deploy (CI, binary) | Small |\n\nPhases 1-3 produce a fully functional standalone `bf` that replaces `br`. Phase 4 adds atomic claiming for fleet operations. Phase 4B adds extended features that make bf more powerful than br. Phases 5-6 integrate and deploy.\n\n---\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **SQLite-primary, JSONL as git artifact**: SQLite is the live database — all reads and writes go through it. JSONL is exported on `bf sync` for git commit, providing backup, cross-machine sharing, and recovery. This matches how bd and br work, but with rusqlite (no corruption) and `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for atomic claim (no race condition).\n\n2. **rusqlite, not FrankenSQLite**: We already proved in the beads_rust fork that FrankenSQLite has corruption issues. Start clean with rusqlite.\n\n3. **`BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for atomic claim**: The read-score-update sequence runs in a single SQLite write transaction. No flock, no streaming rewrite, no temp files. SQLite's own locking serializes concurrent claims correctly.\n\n4. **No automatic git commits**: Match br's explicit, non-invasive philosophy. Users run `bf sync` when they want to commit JSONL changes.\n\n5. **Cattle, not pets**: Workers are disposable fleet units with no identity, no memory, and no conversation. Claims are atomic SQL dequeues from a priority queue, not negotiations.\n\n6. **Stale reclamation as a side effect of claiming**: The first statement in every `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` claim transaction resets expired `in_progress` beads. No separate heartbeat system, no background process.\n\n7. **Server as future optimization, not v1**: A persistent in-memory server would reduce per-claim latency from ~2ms to ~0.1ms by eliminating SQLite file I/O. Premature for current scale — can be added transparently later.\n\n8. **Rotation keeps JSONL artifact lean**: Closed beads older than `rotate_age_days` are exported to archive files (`issues.jsonl.1`, etc.) rather than the active `issues.jsonl`. Keeps the committed artifact small and git diffs readable.\n\n9. **Annotations as extension mechanism**: Arbitrary `BTreeMap<String, String>` on beads lets external tools (NEEDLE, custom scripts) store structured metadata without schema changes. Stored in a separate `bead_annotations` table — never as a column on `issues` — because br's `issues_column_order_matches()` triggers `rebuild_issues_table()` on any column count mismatch, silently destroying any extra column on every br open. br never reads or writes `bead_annotations`, so annotation data survives any br operation unchanged.\n\n10. **Events table for history**: Every mutation records an event row. `bf log` queries the events table — O(1) by issue ID. Long-term history via git log on `issues.jsonl`.\n\n11. **Secret scanning before write**: Built-in regex-based secret detection prevents credentials from entering SQLite or the JSONL export. Configurable patterns, git pre-commit hook integration.\n\n12. **`.beads/` is committed to git**: `issues.jsonl` + config are version-controlled. `beads.db` is gitignored (binary, rebuilt from JSONL on `bf sync --import`). Secret scanning protects the exported JSONL.\n\n13. **Critical path drives claim priority, not just listed priority**: Listed priority is a human signal, but it decays as the project evolves and humans stop curating it. Critical path float is computed from the live dependency graph and is always current. A priority-3 bead on the critical path outranks a priority-0 bead with float=5 — the fleet naturally works on what matters most without human recuration.\n\n14. **Worker composition is first-class metadata**: `--model`, `--harness`, `--harness-version` on `bf claim` record what kind of agent did the work. This transforms the events table from a bare audit log into a performance dataset: which model+harness combinations close which task types fastest. The fleet becomes self-optimizing — velocity data informs which workers to dispatch to which workspace, without any external ML pipeline.\n\n---\n\n## Alternative Approaches Considered\n\n### In-Memory Server (deferred)\n\nThe plan originally included a coordination server: persistent process, Unix socket protocol, `Arc<Mutex<ServerState>>`, in-memory BTreeMap priority queues, heartbeat-based stale detection. This was deferred to a future optimization because SQLite `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` delivers sufficient throughput at current scale.\n\nThe server would reduce per-claim latency from ~2ms (SQLite transaction) to ~0.1ms (memory pop). This matters only if fleets exceed 50 workers or claim frequency exceeds ~500/sec — neither is on the horizon. The server can be added transparently: `bf claim` detects the server socket, delegates if present, falls back to SQLite if not.\n\n### MCP Agent Mail (Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail_rust)\n\nJeffrey Emanuel's companion system for inter-agent coordination. A Rust MCP server with 36 tools, 33 resources, Git-backed archive, SQLite indexing, HTTP/TUI/Robot surfaces.\n\n**Architecture**: Agents register identities, claim file reservations with TTL, exchange threaded messages, negotiate work assignments via acknowledgements. Coordination happens through conversation — \"I'm taking src/auth/**, can you work on tests/?\"\n\n**Why it doesn't fit bead-forge**: Agent Mail is built for the **pets** model — named, persistent agents that maintain identity across sessions, negotiate via messaging, and coordinate through conversation. NEEDLE uses the **cattle** model — disposable workers that are interchangeable, stateless, and need atomic dequeue without any conversation.\n\n| Aspect | Agent Mail (Pets) | bead-forge (Cattle) |\n|--------|-------------------|---------------------|\n| Agent identity | Registered, named, persistent | Anonymous, stateless, disposable |\n| Work assignment | Negotiation, messaging, agreement | Atomic SQL dequeue (BEGIN IMMEDIATE) |\n| Coordination mechanism | Threaded messages, acknowledgements | Kernel-provided mutual exclusion |\n| File/workspace ownership | Advisory file reservations | None — beads are the unit of work |\n| Communication pattern | Multi-turn conversation | Single CLI invocation |\n| Failure mode | Agent re-joins conversation | Agent dies, claim TTL expires, bead reopens |\n| Agent count | Small (3-5 named agents) | Large fleet (11-20+ disposable workers) |\n\n**What to borrow from Agent Mail**:\n- **TTL-based claim expiration**: Agent Mail uses TTL on file reservations. bead-forge applies the same concept — claims older than `claim_ttl` are reclaimed to `open` on the next claim invocation.\n\n**What to explicitly reject**:\n- **Agent identities**: Workers don't register or maintain identity. The `--assignee` field is for logging, not identity.\n- **Messaging/threading**: Workers don't send messages to each other. The only communication is claim/close through the JSONL file.\n- **File reservations**: Workers operate on entire beads, not file-level reservations.\n- **MCP layer**: bead-forge is a CLI. No stdio-based MCP protocol.\n- **Advisory locks**: Claims are enforced by `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, not advisory. Once claimed (inside the transaction), no other worker sees that bead as open.\n\n### BeadHub (juanre/beadhub)\n\nA coordination server for AI agent teams built on top of Steve Yegge's Dolt-based `bd`. The `bdh` CLI wraps `bd` and adds work claiming, file reservation, presence awareness, and inter-agent messaging. Includes a web dashboard.\n\n**Why it doesn't fit**: Tied to Dolt-based `bd` architecture (not JSONL). bead-forge needs JSONL+SQLite compatibility with br, not Dolt compatibility with bd. However, the coordination server concept validates that this problem is real and worth solving.\n\n### Beads Viewer (Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer)\n\nGraph-aware TUI with PageRank-based prioritization, critical path analysis, and kanban board view.\n\n**Why it doesn't fit**: Read-only visualization tool. Cannot perform writes (claiming, status changes). The PageRank and critical path concepts are interesting for future prioritization but don't solve the concurrency problem.\n","numLines":1560,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1560}}}

)?;\n271\t            }\n272\t            Ok(())\n273\t        })\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    pub fn update_issue(&self, id: &str, changes: &IssueChanges) -> Result<()> {\n277\t        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n278\t            let mut updates = Vec::new();\n279\t            let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n280\t            if let Some(ref title) = changes.title {\n281\t                updates.push(\"title = ?\");\n282\t                params.push(Box::new(title.clone()));\n283\t            }\n284\t            if let Some(ref description) = changes.description {\n285\t                updates.push(\"description = ?\");\n286\t                params.push(Box::new(description.clone()));\n287\t            }\n288\t            if let Some(ref design) = changes.design {\n289\t                updates.push(\"design = ?\");\n290\t                params.push(Box::new(design.clone()));\n291\t            }\n292\t            if let Some(ref acceptance_criteria) = changes.acceptance_criteria {\n293\t                updates.push(\"acceptance_criteria = ?\");\n294\t                params.push(Box::new(acceptance_criteria.clone()));\n295\t            }\n296\t            if let Some(ref notes) = changes.notes {\n297\t                updates.push(\"notes = ?\");\n298\t                params.push(Box::new(notes.clone()));\n299\t            }\n300\t            if let Some(ref status) = changes.status {\n301\t                updates.push(\"status = ?\");\n302\t                params.push(Box::new(status.to_string()));\n303\t            }\n304\t            if let Some(priority) = changes.priority {\n305\t                updates.push(\"priority = ?\");\n306\t                params.push(Box::new(priority));\n307\t            }\n308\t            if let Some(ref issue_type) = changes.issue_type {\n309\t                updates.push(\"issue_type = ?\");\n310\t                params.push(Box::new(issue_type.to_string()));\n311\t            }\n312\t            if let Some(ref assignee) = changes.assignee {\n313\t                updates.push(\"assignee = ?\");\n314\t                params.push(Box::new(assignee.clone()));\n315\t            }\n316\t            if let Some(ref owner) = changes.owner {\n317\t                updates.push(\"owner = ?\");\n318\t                params.push(Box::new(owner.clone()));\n319\t            }\n320\t            if let Some(estimated_minutes) = changes.estimated_minutes {\n321\t                updates.push(\"estimated_minutes = ?\");\n322\t                params.push(Box::new(estimated_minutes));\n323\t            }\n324\t            if let Some(ref due_at) = changes.due_at {\n325\t                updates.push(\"due_at = ?\");\n326\t                params.push(Box::new(due_at.to_rfc3339()));\n327\t            }\n328\t            if let Some(ref defer_until) = changes.defer_until {\n329\t                updates.push(\"defer_until = ?\");\n330\t                params.push(Box::new(defer_until.to_rfc3339()));\n331\t            }\n332\t            if let Some(ref external_ref) = changes.external_ref {\n333\t                updates.push(\"external_ref = ?\");\n334\t                params.push(Box::new(external_ref.clone()));\n335\t            }\n336\t            let now = Utc::now();\n337\t            if !updates.is_empty() {\n338\t                updates.push(\"updated_at = ?\");\n339\t                params.push(Box::new(now.to_rfc3339()));\n340\t                let query = format!(\"UPDATE issues SET {} WHERE id = ?\", updates.join(\", \"));\n341\t                let mut all_params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = params.into_iter().collect();\n342\t                all_params.push(Box::new(id.to_string()));\n343\t                let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n344\t                    all_params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n345\t                tx.execute(&query, param_refs.as_slice())?;\n346\t            }\n347\t            // Handle label updates separately\n348\t            if let Some(ref labels) = changes.labels {\n349\t                tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n350\t                for label in labels {\n351\t                    tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\", params![id, label])?;\n352\t                }\n353\t            }\n354\t            // Handle annotation updates separately\n355\t            if let Some(ref annotations) = changes.annotations {\n356\t                tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM bead_annotations WHERE bead_id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n357\t                for (key, value) in annotations {\n358\t                    tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO bead_annotations (bead_id, key, value) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\", params![id, key, value])?;\n359\t                }\n360\t            }\n361\t            // Mark as dirty for export (if any changes were made)\n362\t            if !updates.is_empty() || changes.labels.is_some() || changes.annotations.is_some() {\n363\t                tx.execute(\n364\t                    \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n365\t                    params![id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n366\t                )?;\n367\t            }\n368\t            Ok(())\n369\t        })\n370\t    }\n371\t\n372\t    /// Update an issue from JSONL import data.\n373\t    ///\n374\t    /// This replaces all fields of the existing issue with the imported data,\n375\t    /// used during JSONL import when the content_hash differs.\n376\t    pub fn update_issue_from_json(&self, issue: &Issue) -> Result<()> {\n377\t ?, updated_at = ?         Ok(())\n465\t        })\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    pub fn mark_dirty(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n469\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n470\t        let now = )?;\n475\t        Ok(())\n476\t    }\n477\t\n478\t    pub fn rebuild_blocked_cache(&self) -> Result<()> {\n479\t        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n480\t            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache\", [])?;\n481\t\n482\t            tx.execute(\n483\t                \"INSERT INTO blocked_issues_cache (issue_id, blocked_by, blocked_at)\n484\t                 SELECT d.issue_id, '[' || GROUP_CONCAT('\\\"' || d.depends_on_id || '\\\"') || ']' AS blocked_by, ?1\n485\t                 FROM dependencies d\n486\t                 INNER JOIN issues i ON i.id = d.depends_on_id\n487\t                 WHERE d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n488\t                 AND i.status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')\n489\t                 GROUP BY d.issue_id\",\n490\t                params![Utc::now().to_rfc3339()],\n491\t            )?;\n492\t            Ok(())\n493\t        })\n494\t    }\n495\t\n496\t    pub fn        Ok(count as usize)\n500\t    }\n501\t\n502\t    pub fn sync_from_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<ImportResult> {\n503\t        import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n504\t            let                }\n510\t                Some(_) => Ok(false),\n511\t            }\n512\t        })\n513\t    }\n514\t\n515\t    pub fn sync_to_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path, dirty_only: bool) -> Ok(result.count)\n519\t        } else {\n520\t            let result = export_jsonl(jsonl_path, || self.list_all_issues())?;\n521\t            Ok(result.count)\n522\t        }\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    fn row_to_issue_conn(conn: &Connection, row: &rusqlite::Row) -> Result<Issue> {\n526\t        let status_str: String = row.get(7)?;\n527\t        let type_str: String = row.get(9)?;\n528\t        let parse_opt_dt = |idx: usize| -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>> {\n529\t            let s: Option<String> = row.get(idx)?;\n530\t            match s {\n531\t                None => Ok(None),\n532\t                Some(ref val) if val.is_empty() => Ok(None),\n533\t                Some(val) => Ok(Some(parse_datetime(val)?)),\n534\t            }\n535\t        };\n536\t        let id: String = row.get(0)?;\n537\t        Ok(Issue {\n538\t            content_hash: row.get(1)?,\n539\t            title: row.get(2)?,\n540\t            description: row.get(3)?,\n541\t            design: row.get(4)?,\n542\t            acceptance_criteria: row.get(5)?,\n543\t            notes: row.get(6)?,\n544\t            status: Status::from_str(&status_str).unwrap_or(Status::Custom(status_str)),\n545\t            priority: row.get(8)?,\n546\t            issue_type: IssueType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(IssueType::Custom(type_str)),\n547\t            assignee: row.get(10)?,\n548\t            owner: row.get(11)?,\n549\t            estimated_minutes: row.get(12)?,\n550\t            created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(13)?)?,\n551\t            created_by: row.get(14)?,\n552\t            updated_at: parse_datetime(row.get(15)?)?,\n553\t            closed_at: parse_opt_dt(16)?,\n554\t            close_reason: row.get(17)?,\n555\t            closed_by_session: row.get(18)?,\n556\t            due_at: parse_opt_dt(19)?,\n557\t            defer_until: parse_opt_dt(20)?,\n558\t            external_ref: row.get(21)?,\n559\t            source_system: row.get(22)?,\n560\t            source_repo: row.get(23)?,\n561\t            deleted_at: parse_opt_dt(24)?,\n562\t            deleted_by: row.get(25)?,\n563\t            delete_reason: row.get(26)?,\n564\t            original_type: row.get(27)?,\n565\t            compaction_level: row.get(28)?,\n566\t            compacted_at: parse_opt_dt(29)?,\n567\t            compacted_at_commit: row.get(30)?,\n568\t            original_size: row.get(31)?,\n569\t            sender: row.get(32)?,\n570\t            ephemeral: row.get::<_, i32>(33)? != 0,\n571\t            pinned: row.get::<_, i32>(34)? != 0,\n572\t            is_template: row.get::<_, i32>(35)? != 0,\n573\t            labels: Self::load_labels_conn(conn, &id)?,\n574\t            dependencies: Self::load_dependencies_conn(conn, &id)?,\n575\t            comments: Self::load_comments_conn(conn, &id)?,\n576\t            annotations: Self::load_annotations_conn(conn, &id)?,\n577\t            id,\n578\t        })\n579\t    }\n580\t\n581\t    fn load_labels_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n582\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\"SELECT label FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\")?;\n583\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n584\t        let mut labels = Vec::new();\n585\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n586\t            labels.push(row.get(0)?);\n587\t        }\n588\t        Ok(labels)\n589\t    }\n590\t\n591\t    fn load_dependencies_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n592\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n593\t            \"SELECT issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?1\",\n594\t        )?;\n595\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n596\t        let mut deps = Vec::new();\n597\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n598\t            let type_str: String = row.get(2)?;\n599\t            deps.push(Dependency {\n600\t                issue_id: row.get(0)?,\n601\t                depends_on_id: row.get(1)?,\n602\t                dep_type: DependencyType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(DependencyType::Custom(type_str)),\n603\t                metadata: row.get::<_, Option<String>>(3)?.and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok()),\n604\t                thread_id: row.get(4)?,\n605\t                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(5)?)?,\n606\t                created_by: row.get(6)?,\n607\t            });\n608\t        }\n609\t        Ok(deps)\n610\t    }\n611\t\n612\t    fn load_comments_conn(conn: ?1\",\n615\t        )?;\n616\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n617\t        let mut comments = Vec::new();\n618\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n619\t            comments.push(Comment {\n620\t                id: row.get(0)?,\n621\t                issue_id: row.get(1)?,\n622\t                author: row.get(2)?,\n623\t                body: row.get(3)?,\n624\t                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(4)?)?,\n625\t            });\n626\t        }\n627\t        Ok(comments)\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    fn load_annotations_conn(conn: &Connection,   let value: String = row.get(1)?;\n637\t            annotations.insert(key, value);\n638\t        }\n639\t        Ok(annotations)\n640\t    }\n641\t\n642\t    fn load_labels(&self, issue_id: &str) -> }\n661\t\n662\t    pub fn add_dependency(&self, issue_id: &str, depends_on_id: &str, dep_type: &DependencyType, created_by: &str) -> Result<()> {\n663\t        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&Transaction) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n        match f(&tx) {\n            Ok(result) => {\n                tx.commit()?;\n                drop(conn);\n                Ok(result)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let _ = tx.rollback();\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    pub fn with_immediate_transaction<T, F>(&self, f: F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: Fn(&Connection) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let mut attempt = 0;\n        loop {\n            let outcome = {\n                let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n                match conn.execute_batch(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE\") {\n                    Err(e) if is_busy_error(&e) && attempt < MAX_RETRIES => None,\n                    Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n                    Ok(_) => {\n                        let r = f(&conn);\n                        match &r {\n                            Ok(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"COMMIT\"); }\n                            Err(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"ROLLBACK\"); }\n                        }\n                        Some(r)\n                    }\n                }\n            };\n            match outcome {\n                Some(r) => return r,\n                None => {\n                    attempt += 1;\n                    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(RETRY_BASE_MS * attempt as u64));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub &issue.created_at.to_rfc3339(), &issue.created_by,\n                    &issue.updated_at.to_rfc3339(), issue.closed_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()),\n                    &issue.close_reason, &issue.closed_by_session, issue.due_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()),\n                    issue.defer_until.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.external_ref, &issue.source_system,\n                    issue.source_repo.as_deref().unwrap_or(\".\"),\n                    issue.deleted_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.deleted_by,\n                    &issue.delete_reason, &issue.original_type, &issue.compaction_level,\n                    issue.compacted_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.compacted_at_commit,\n                    &issue.original_size, &issue.sender,\n                    if issue.ephemeral { 1 } else { 0 },\n                    if issue.pinned { 1 } else { 0 },\n                    if issue.is_template { 1 } else { 0 },\n                ],\n            )?;\n            for label in &issue.labels {\n                tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\", params![&issue.id, label])?;\n            }\n            for dep in &issue.dependencies {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by)\n                     VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)\",\n                    params![\n                        &dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id, &dep.dep_type.to_string(),\n                        dep.metadata.as_ref().map(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).ok()).flatten(),\n                        &dep.thread_id, &dep.created_at.to_rfc3339(), &dep.created_by,\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n            for comment in &issue.comments {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO comments (id, issue_id, author, text, created_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n                    params![\n                        &comment.id, &comment.issue_id, &comment.author, &comment.body,\n                        &comment.created_at.to_rfc3339(),\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n            for (key, value) in &issue.annotations {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO bead_annotations (bead_id, key, value) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n                    params![&issue.id, key, value],\n                )?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn update_issue(&self, id: &str, changes: &IssueChanges) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let mut updates = Vec::new();\n            let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n            if let Some(ref title) = changes.title {\n                updates.push(\"title = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(title.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref description) = changes.description {\n                updates.push(\"description = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(description.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref design) = changes.design {\n                updates.push(\"design = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(design.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref acceptance_criteria) = changes.acceptance_criteria {\n                updates.push(\"acceptance_criteria = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(acceptance_criteria.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref notes) = changes.notes {\n                updates.push(\"notes = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(notes.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref status) = changes.status {\n                updates.push(\"status = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(status.to_string()));\n            }\n            if let Some(priority) = changes.priority {\n                updates.push(\"priority = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(priority));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref issue_type) = changes.issue_type {\n                updates.push(\"issue_type = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(issue_type.to_string()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref assignee) = changes.assignee {\n                updates.push(\"assignee = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(assignee.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref owner) = changes.owner {\n                updates.push(\"owner = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(owner.clone()));\n            }\n            if let Some(estimated_minutes) = changes.estimated_minutes {\n                updates.push(\"estimated_minutes = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(estimated_minutes));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref due_at) = changes.due_at {\n                updates.push(\"due_at = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(due_at.to_rfc3339()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref defer_until) = changes.defer_until {\n                updates.push(\"defer_until = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(defer_until.to_rfc3339()));\n            }\n            if let Some(ref external_ref) = changes.external_ref {\n                updates.push(\"external_ref = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(external_ref.clone()));\n            }\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            if !updates.is_empty() {\n                updates.push(\"updated_at = ?\");\n                params.push(Box::new(now.to_rfc3339()));\n                let query = format!(\"UPDATE issues SET {} WHERE id = ?\", updates.join(\", \"));\n                let mut all_params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = params.into_iter().collect();\n                all_params.push(Box::new(id.to_string()));\n                let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n                    all_params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n                tx.execute(&query, param_refs.as_slice())?;\n            }\n            // Handle label updates separately\n            if let Some(ref labels) = changes.labels {\n                tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n                for label in labels {\n                    tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\", params![id, label])?;\n                }\n            }\n            // Handle annotation updates separately\n            if let Some(ref annotations) = changes.annotations {\n                tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM bead_annotations WHERE bead_id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n                for (key, value) in annotations {\n                    tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO bead_annotations (bead_id, key, value) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\", params![id, key, value])?;\n                }\n            }\n            // Mark as dirty for export (if any changes were made)\n            if !updates.is_empty() || changes.labels.is_some() || changes.annotations.is_some() {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n                    params![id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n                )?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update an issue from JSONL import data.\n    ///\n    /// This replaces all fields of the existing issue with the imported data,\n    /// used during JSONL import when the content_hash differs.\n    pub fn update_issue_from_json(&self, issue: &Issue) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            // Delete existing related data\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\", params![&issue.id])?;\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?1\", params![&issue.id])?;\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM comments WHERE issue_id = ?1\", params![&issue.id])?;\n\n            // Update the issue row with all fields\n            tx.execute(\n                \"UPDATE issues SET\n                    content_hash = ?1, title = ?2, description = ?3, design = ?4,\n                    acceptance_criteria = ?5, notes = ?6, status = ?7, priority = ?8,\n                    issue_type = ?9, assignee = ?10, owner = ?11, estimated_minutes = ?12,\n                    created_at = ?13, created_by = ?14, updated_at = ?15, closed_at = ?16,\n                    close_reason = ?17, closed_by_session = ?18, due_at = ?19, defer_until = ?20,\n                    external_ref = ?21, source_system = ?22, source_repo = ?23,\n                    deleted_at = ?24, deleted_by = ?25, delete_reason = ?26, original_type = ?27,\n                    compaction_level = ?28, compacted_at = ?29, compacted_at_commit = ?30,\n                    original_size = ?31, sender = ?32, ephemeral = ?33, pinned = ?34, is_template = ?35\n                 WHERE id = ?36\",\n                params![\n                    &issue.content_hash, &issue.title,\n                    issue.description.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                    issue.design.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                    issue.acceptance_criteria.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                    issue.notes.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                    &issue.status.to_string(),\n                    &issue.priority, &issue.issue_type.to_string(), &issue.assignee, &issue.owner,\n                    &issue.estimated_minutes, &issue.created_at.to_rfc3339(), &issue.created_by,\n                    &issue.updated_at.to_rfc3339(), issue.closed_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()),\n                    &issue.close_reason, &issue.closed_by_session, issue.due_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()),\n                    issue.defer_until.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.external_ref, &issue.source_system,\n                    issue.source_repo.as_deref().unwrap_or(\".\"),\n                    issue.deleted_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.deleted_by,\n                    &issue.delete_reason, &issue.original_type, &issue.compaction_level,\n                    issue.compacted_at.map(|d| d.to_rfc3339()), &issue.compacted_at_commit,\n                    &issue.original_size, &issue.sender,\n                    if issue.ephemeral { 1 } else { 0 },\n                    if issue.pinned { 1 } else { 0 },\n                    if issue.is_template { 1 } else { 0 },\n                    &issue.id,\n                ],\n            )?;\n\n            // Re-insert labels, dependencies, and comments\n            for label in &issue.labels {\n                tx.execute(\"INSERT INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\", params![&issue.id, label])?;\n            }\n            for dep in &issue.dependencies {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by)\n                     VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)\",\n                    params![\n                        &dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id, &dep.dep_type.to_string(),\n                        dep.metadata.as_ref().map(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).ok()).flatten(),\n                        &dep.thread_id, &dep.created_at.to_rfc3339(), &dep.created_by,\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n            for comment in &issue.comments {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO comments (id, issue_id, author, text, created_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n                    params![\n                        &comment.id, &comment.issue_id, &comment.author, &comment.body,\n                        &comment.created_at.to_rfc3339(),\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn close_issue(&self, id: &str, reason: &str, actor: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"UPDATE issues SET status = 'closed', closed_at = ?, close_reason = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                params![now.to_rfc3339(), reason, now.to_rfc3339(), id],\n            )?;\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, old_value, new_value, created_at) VALUES (?1, 'closed', ?2, NULL, ?3, ?4)\",\n                params![id, actor, reason, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n                params![id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn mark_dirty(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n            params![id, now],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    pub fn rebuild_blocked_cache(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache\", [])?;\n\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO blocked_issues_cache (issue_id, blocked_by, blocked_at)\n                 SELECT d.issue_id, '[' || GROUP_CONCAT('\\\"' || d.depends_on_id || '\\\"') || ']' AS blocked_by, ?1\n                 FROM dependencies d\n                 INNER JOIN issues i ON i.id = d.depends_on_id\n                 WHERE d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n                 AND i.status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')\n                 GROUP BY d.issue_id\",\n                params![Utc::now().to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn count_issues(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(count as usize)\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_from_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<ImportResult> {\n        import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n            let existing = self.get_issue(&issue.id)?;\n            match existing {\n                None => {\n                    self.create_issue(issue)?;\n                    Ok(true)\n                }\n                Some(_) => Ok(false),\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_to_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path, dirty_only: bool) -> Result<usize> {\n        if dirty_only {\n            let result = export_jsonl_dirty(jsonl_path, || self.list_dirty_issues(), || self.clear_dirty())?;\n            Ok(result.count)\n        } else {\n            let result = export_jsonl(jsonl_path, || self.list_all_issues())?;\n            Ok(result.count)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn row_to_issue_conn(conn: &Connection, row: &rusqlite::Row) -> Result<Issue> {\n        let status_str: String = row.get(7)?;\n        let type_str: String = row.get(9)?;\n        let parse_opt_dt = |idx: usize| -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>> {\n            let s: Option<String> = row.get(idx)?;\n            match s {\n                None => Ok(None),\n                Some(ref val) if val.is_empty() => Ok(None),\n                Some(val) => Ok(Some(parse_datetime(val)?)),\n            }\n        };\n        let id: String = row.get(0)?;\n        Ok(Issue {\n            content_hash: row.get(1)?,\n            title: row.get(2)?,\n            description: row.get(3)?,\n            design: row.get(4)?,\n            acceptance_criteria: row.get(5)?,\n            notes: row.get(6)?,\n            status: Status::from_str(&status_str).unwrap_or(Status::Custom(status_str)),\n            priority: row.get(8)?,\n            issue_type: IssueType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(IssueType::Custom(type_str)),\n            assignee: row.get(10)?,\n            owner: row.get(11)?,\n            estimated_minutes: row.get(12)?,\n            created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(13)?)?,\n            created_by: row.get(14)?,\n            updated_at: parse_datetime(row.get(15)?)?,\n            closed_at: parse_opt_dt(16)?,\n            close_reason: row.get(17)?,\n            closed_by_session: row.get(18)?,\n            due_at: parse_opt_dt(19)?,\n            defer_until: parse_opt_dt(20)?,\n            external_ref: row.get(21)?,\n            source_system: row.get(22)?,\n            source_repo: row.get(23)?,\n            deleted_at: parse_opt_dt(24)?,\n            deleted_by: row.get(25)?,\n            delete_reason: row.get(26)?,\n            original_type: row.get(27)?,\n            compaction_level: row.get(28)?,\n            compacted_at: parse_opt_dt(29)?,\n            compacted_at_commit: row.get(30)?,\n            original_size: row.get(31)?,\n            sender: row.get(32)?,\n            ephemeral: row.get::<_, i32>(33)? != 0,\n            pinned: row.get::<_, i32>(34)? != 0,\n            is_template: row.get::<_, i32>(35)? != 0,\n            labels: Self::load_labels_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            dependencies: Self::load_dependencies_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            comments: Self::load_comments_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            annotations: Self::load_annotations_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            id,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn load_labels_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\"SELECT label FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\")?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut labels = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            labels.push(row.get(0)?);\n        }\n        Ok(labels)\n    }\n\n    fn load_dependencies_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut deps = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            let type_str: String = row.get(2)?;\n            deps.push(Dependency {\n                issue_id: row.get(0)?,\n                depends_on_id: row.get(1)?,\n                dep_type: DependencyType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(DependencyType::Custom(type_str)),\n                metadata: row.get::<_, Option<String>>(3)?.and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok()),\n                thread_id: row.get(4)?,\n                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(5)?)?,\n                created_by: row.get(6)?,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(deps)\n    }\n\n    fn load_comments_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Comment>> {\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, issue_id, author, text, created_at FROM comments WHERE issue_id = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut comments = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            comments.push(Comment {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                issue_id: row.get(1)?,\n                author: row.get(2)?,\n                body: row.get(3)?,\n                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(4)?)?,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(comments)\n    }\n\n    fn load_annotations_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) -> Result<BTreeMap<String, String>> {\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\"SELECT key, value FROM bead_annotations WHERE bead_id = ?1\")?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut annotations = BTreeMap::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            let key: String = row.get(0)?;\n            let value: String = row.get(1)?;\n            annotations.insert(key, value);\n        }\n        Ok(annotations)\n    }\n\n    fn load_labels(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Self::load_labels_conn(&conn, issue_id)\n    }\n\n    fn load_dependencies(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Self::load_dependencies_conn(&conn, issue_id)\n    }\n\n    fn load_comments(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Comment>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Self::load_comments_conn(&conn, issue_id)\n    }\n\n    fn load_annotations(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<BTreeMap<String, String>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Self::load_annotations_conn(&conn, issue_id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn add_dependency(&self, issue_id: &str, depends_on_id: &str, dep_type: &DependencyType, created_by: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, created_at, created_by) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n                params![issue_id, depends_on_id, dep_type.to_string(), now.to_rfc3339(), created_by],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn remove_dependency(&self, issue_id: &str, depends_on_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?1 AND depends_on_id = ?2\", params![issue_id, depends_on_id])?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_dependencies(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n        self.load_dependencies(issue_id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_dependents(&self, depends_on_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by FROM dependencies WHERE depends_on_id = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![depends_on_id])?;\n        let mut deps = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            let type_str: String = row.get(2)?;\n            deps.push(Dependency {\n                issue_id: row.get(0)?,\n                depends_on_id: row.get(1)?,\n                dep_type: DependencyType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(DependencyType::Custom(type_str)),\n                metadata: row.get::<_, Option<String>>(3)?.and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok()),\n                thread_id: row.get(4)?,\n                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(5)?)?,\n                created_by: row.get(6)?,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(deps)\n    }\n\n    pub fn add_label(&self, issue_id: &str, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\", params![issue_id, label])?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn remove_label(&self, issue_id: &str, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1 AND label = ?2\", params![issue_id, label])?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_labels(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        self.load_labels(issue_id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn list_all_labels(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, i64)>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\"SELECT label, COUNT(*) as count FROM labels GROUP BY label ORDER BY count DESC\")?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n        let mut labels = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            labels.push((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?));\n        }\n        Ok(labels)\n    }\n\n    pub fn add_comment(&self, issue_id: &str, author: &str, body: &str) -> Result<i64> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO comments (issue_id, author, text, created_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)\",\n                params![issue_id, author, body, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            Ok(tx.last_insert_rowid())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn list_comments(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Comment>> {\n        self.load_comments(issue_id)\n    }\n\n    // Annotation methods\n    pub fn get_annotations(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<BTreeMap<String, String>> {\n        self.load_annotations(issue_id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_annotation(&self, issue_id: &str, key: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO bead_annotations (bead_id, key, value) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n                params![issue_id, key, value],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn remove_annotation(&self, issue_id: &str, key: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            tx.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM bead_annotations WHERE bead_id = ?1 AND key = ?2\",\n                params![issue_id, key],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn clear_annotations(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\"DELETE FROM bead_annotations WHERE bead_id = ?1\", params![issue_id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    pub fn search_issues(&self, query: Option<&str>, status: &[Status], issue_type: &[IssueType], assignee: Option<&str>, labels: &[String], priority_min: Option<i32>, priority_max: Option<i32>, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Issue>> {\n        let mut sql = String::from(\n            \"SELECT DISTINCT i.id, i.content_hash, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,\n                    i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.owner, i.estimated_minutes,\n                    i.created_at, i.created_by, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.close_reason,\n                    i.closed_by_session, i.due_at, i.defer_until, i.external_ref, i.source_system,\n                    i.source_repo, i.deleted_at, i.deleted_by, i.delete_reason, i.original_type,\n                    i.compaction_level, i.compacted_at, i.compacted_at_commit, i.original_size,\n                    i.sender, i.ephemeral, i.pinned, i.is_template\n             FROM issues i\n             LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n        );\n        let mut params = Vec::new();\n        let mut param_idx = 1;\n        if let Some(q) = query {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND (i.title LIKE ?{} OR i.description LIKE ?{})\", param_idx, param_idx + 1));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            param_idx += 2;\n        }\n        for s in status {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(s.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        for t in issue_type {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(t.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(a.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if !labels.is_empty() {\n            let label_conditions: Vec<String> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, _)| format!(\"l.label = ?{}\", param_idx + i)).collect();\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n            for label in labels {\n                params.push(label.clone());\n                param_idx += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(min.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(max.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n        if limit > 0 {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n        }\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            issues.push(Self::row_to_issue_conn(&conn, row)?);\n        }\n        Ok(issues)\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let total: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let open: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'open' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let in_progress: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'in_progress' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let closed: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'closed' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(Stats { total: total as usize, open: open as usize, in_progress: in_progress as usize, closed: closed as usize })\n    }\n\n    /// Get the score of the top candidate bead for claiming.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if no candidates are available.\n    /// Used by claim_any() for cross-workspace scoring comparison.\n    pub fn top_candidate_score(&self) -> Result<Option<crate::claim::Score>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                    COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                    i.priority,\n                    strftime('%s', i.created_at) as created_ts\n             FROM issues i\n             LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n             LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n             WHERE i.status = 'open'\n               AND i.ephemeral = 0\n               AND i.pinned = 0\n               AND i.is_template = 0\n               AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n             GROUP BY i.id\n             ORDER BY\n                 downstream_impact DESC,\n                 critical_float ASC,\n                 i.priority ASC,\n                 i.created_at ASC\n             LIMIT 1\",\n        )?;\n\n        let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n        if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            Ok(Some(crate::claim::Score {\n                downstream_impact: row.get(0)?,\n                critical_float: row.get(1)?,\n                priority: row.get(2)?,\n                created_at_ts: row.get(3)?,\n            }))\n        } else {\n            Ok(None)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a worker session for tracking metadata.\n    ///\n    /// Stores worker metadata (model, harness, version) for each claim operation.\n    /// Used by velocity-aware scoring and audit trails.\n    pub fn record_worker_session(\n        &self,\n        worker_id: &str,\n        model: Option<&str>,\n        harness: Option<&str>,\n        harness_version: Option<&str>,\n        bead_id: &str,\n        workspace_path: &str,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO worker_sessions (worker_id, model, harness, harness_version, bead_id, workspace_path)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)\",\n            params![\n                worker_id,\n                model,\n                harness,\n                harness_version,\n                bead_id,\n                workspace_path,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]\npub struct Stats {\n    pub total: usize,\n    pub open: usize,\n    pub in_progress: usize,\n    pub closed: usize,\n}\n\nfn is_busy_error(e: &rusqlite::Error) -> bool {\n    matches!(e, rusqlite::Error::SqliteFailure(rusqlite::ffi::Error { code: rusqlite::ErrorCode::DatabaseBusy, .. }, _))\n}\n\nfn parse_datetime(s: String) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>> {\n run_cli() -> Result<Cli> {\n488\t    Ok(Cli::try_parse()?)\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\tpub fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<()> {\n492\t    let workspace = cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n493\t\n494\t    // For init command, we allow the .beads directory to not exist yet\n495\t    match &cli.command {\n496\t        Commands::Init { .. } => {\n497\t unreachable!(),\n501\t            };\n502\t        }\n503\t        _ => {}\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(&workspace)\n507\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads type_, assignee, priority, limit, format, json } => {\n515\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n516\t            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n517\t        }\n518\t        Commands::Show { id, format, json } => {\n519\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n520\t            cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format)\n521\t        }\n522\t        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n523\t            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n524\t        }\n525\t        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n526\t        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n527\t        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n528\t        Commands::Ready { limit, format, json } => {\n529\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n530\t            cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format)\n531\t        }\n532\t        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, any, fallback, workspace_paths, dry_run, format, json } => {\n533\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n534\t            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, any, fallback, &workspace_paths, dry_run, &format)\n535\t        }\n536\t        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n537\t        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n538\t        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n539\t        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n540\t        Commands::Mitosis { id, children, reason, format } => cmd_mitosis(&beads_dir, &id, &children, &reason, &format),\n541\t        Commands::Dep(dep) => cmd_dep(&beads_dir, dep),\n542\t        Commands::Label(label) => cmd_label(&beads_dir, label),\n543\t        Commands::Comments(comments) => cmd_comments(&beads_dir, comments),\n544\t        Commands::Search { query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, format } => {\n545\t            cmd_search(&beads_dir, query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, &format)\n546\t        }\n547\t        Commands::Stats { by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, format } => {\n548\t            cmd_stats(&beads_dir, by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, &format)\n549\t        }\n550\t        Commands::Schema { target, format } => cmd_schema(&target, &format),\n551\t        Commands::Config(config) => cmd_config(&beads_dir, config),\n552\t        Commands::Velocity { model, harness, format } => cmd_velocity(&beads_dir, model, harness, &format),\n553\t        Commands::Labels { id, format } => cmd_labels(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n554\t    }\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\tfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n558\t    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n559\t\n560\t    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n561\t    if !config_path.exists() {\n562\t        let config = format!(\n563\t            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\n564\tdefault_priority: 2\n565\tdefault_type: task\n566\tclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n567\t\"#,\n568\t            prefix\n569\t        );\n570\t        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n574\t    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n575\t        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n576\t        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n580\t    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n581\t\n582\t    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n583\t    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n584\t        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n585\t    }\n586\t\n587\t    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n588\t    Ok(())\n589\t}\n590\t\n591\tfn cmd_create(\n592\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n593\t    title: String,\n594\t    type_: String,\n595\t    priority: i32,\n596\t    description: Option<String>,\n597\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n598\t    labels: Vec<String>,\n599\t) -> Result<()> {\n600\t    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n601\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n602\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n603\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n604\t\n605\t    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n606\t    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n607\t    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n608\t\n609\t    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n610\t    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n611\t    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n612\t    issue.description = description;\n613\t    issue.assignee = assignee;\n614\t    issue.labels = labels;\n615\t\n616\t    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n617\t\n618\t    println!(\"{}\", id);\n619\t    Ok(())\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\tfn cmd_list(\n623\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n624\t    status: Option<String>,\n625\t    type_: Option<String>,\n626\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n627\t    priority: Option<i32>,\n628\t    limit: Option<usize>,\n629\t    format: &str,\n630\t) -> Result<()> {\n631\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n632\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n633\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n634\t\n635\t    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n636\t    if let Some(s) = status {\n637\t        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n638\t    }\n639\t    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n640\t        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n641\t    }\n642\t    filter.assignee = assignee;\n643\t    filter.priority = priority;\n644\t    // --limit 0 means unlimited\n645\t    filter.limit = limit.and_then(|l| if l == 0 { None } else { Some(l) });\n646\t\n647\t    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n648\t\n649\t    match format {\n650\t        \"json\" => {\n651\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issues)?);\n652\t        }\n653\t        _ => {\n654\t            for issue in issues {\n655\t                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n656\t            }\n657\t        }\n658\t    }\n659\t\n660\t    Ok(())\n661\t}\n662\t\n663\tfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n664\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n665\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n666\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n667\t\n668\t    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n669\t\n670\t    match format {\n671\t        \"json\" => {\n672\t            // Strip dependencies/comments before serializing: NEEDLE's BrDependency\n673\t            // format ({id, title, status, priority, dependency_type}) differs from\n674\t            // bead-forge's Dependency format ({issue_id, depends_on_id, type, ...}).\n675\t            // NEEDLE has #[serde(default)] on the deps field so empty is fine.\n676\t            let mut out = issue;\n677\t            out.dependencies = vec![];\n678\t            out.comments = vec![];\n679\t            // Wrap in array so NEEDLE's parse_single_bead (Vec<Bead> → first) works.\n680\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&vec![out])?);\n681\t        }\n682\t        _ => {\n683\t            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n684\t            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n685\t            println!(\"Status: {}\", issue.status);\n686\t            println!(\"Priority: {}\", issue.priority);\n687\t            println!(\"Type: {}\", issue.issue_type);\n688\t            if let Some(desc) = &issue.description {\n689\t                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n690\t            }\n691\t            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n692\t                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n693\t            }\n694\t            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n695\t                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n696\t            }\n697\t        }\n698\t    }\n699\t\n700\t    Ok(())\n701\t}\n702\t\n703\tfn cmd_update(\n704\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n705\t    id: &str,\n706\t    title: Option<String>,\n707\t    status: Option<String>,\n708\t    priority: Option<i32>,\n709\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n710\t) -> Result<()> {\n711\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n712\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n713\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n714\t\n715\t    let mut changes = IssueChanges {\n716\t        title,\n717\t        status: status.map(|s| Status::from_str(&s).ok()).flatten(),\n718\t        priority,\n719\t        assignee,\n720\t        ..Default::default()\n721\t    };\n722\t\n723\t    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n724\t    println!(\"Updated bead {}\", id);\n725\t    Ok(())\n726\t}\n727\t\n728\tfn cmd_close(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n729\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n730\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n731\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n732\t\n733\t    storage.close_issue(id, reason, \"cli\")?;\n734\t    println!(\"Closed bead {}\", id);\n735\t    Ok(())\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\tfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n739\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n740\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n741\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n742\t\n743\t    let changes = IssueChanges {\n744\t Ok(())\n751\t}\n752\t\n753\tfn cmd_delete(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n754\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n755\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n756\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n757\t\n758\t    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n759\t        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n760\t        Ok(())\n761\t    })?;\n762\t\n763\t    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n764\t    Ok(())\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\tfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n768\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n769\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n770\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n771\t\n772\t    let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n773\t        get_ready_candidates(tx, limit)\n774\t    })?;\n775\t\n776\t    match format {\n777\t        \"json\" => {\n778\t            for candidate in candidates {\n779\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&candidate)?);\n780\t            }\n781\t        }\n782\t        _ => {\n783\t            for candidate in candidates {\n784\t                println!(\"[{}] {} (priority={}, impact={})\",\n785\t                    candidate.id, candidate.title, candidate.priority,\n786\t                    candidate.downstream_impact);\n787\t            }\n788\t        }\n789\t    }\n790\t\n791\t    Ok(())\n792\t}\n793\t\n794\tfn cmd_claim(\n795\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n796\t    assignee: &str,\n797\t    model: Option<String>,\n798\t    harness: Option<String>,\n799\t    harness_version: Option<String>,\n800\t    any: bool,\n801\t    fallback: bool,\n802\t    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n803\t    dry_run: bool,\n804\t    format: &str,\n805\t) -> Result<()> {\n806\t    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n807\t    let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n808\t\n809\t    // Build worker metadata\n810\t    let worker_metadata = WorkerMetadata {\n811\t        worker_id: assignee.to_string(),\n812\t        model: model.clone(),\n813\t        harness: harness.clone(),\n814\t        harness_version: harness_version.clone(),\n815\t    };\n816\t\n817\t    if dry_run {\n818\t        // Dry run mode - show what would be claimed\n819\t        let candidates = if any || fallback {\n820\t            // Multi-workspace dry run\n821\t            let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n822\t                // Auto-discover workspaces from current directory\n823\t                find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n824\t            } else {\n825\t                workspace_paths.to_vec()\n826\t            };\n827\t\n828\t            let mut all_candidates = Vec::new();\n829\t            for path in &paths {\n830\t                let local_beads_dir = path.join(\".beads\");\n831\t                if local_beads_dir.exists() {\n832\t                    let local_metadata = match load_metadata(&local_beads_dir) {\n833\t                        Ok(m) => m,\n834\t                        Err(_) => continue,\n835\t                    };\n836\t                    let local_db_path = local_beads_dir.join(&local_metadata.database);\n837\t                    if let Ok(local_storage) = Storage::open(&local_db_path) {\n838\t                        if let Ok(local_candidates) = local_storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n839\t                            get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n840\t                        }) {\n841\t                            for c in local_candidates {\n842\t                                all_candidates.push((path.clone(), c));\n843\t                            }\n844\t                        }\n845\t                    }\n846\t                }\n847\t            }\n848\t\n849\t            // Sort by score and take top 1\n850\t            all_candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n851\t                let score_a = (b.1.downstream_impact, b.1.priority, b.1.created_at.clone());\n852\t                let score_b = (a.1.downstream_impact, a.1.priority, a.1.created_at.clone());\n853\t                score_a.cmp(&score_b)\n854\t            });\n855\t            all_candidates.into_iter().take(1).collect()\n856\t        } else {\n857\t            // Single workspace dry run\n858\t            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n859\t            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n860\t            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n861\t            let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n862\t                get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n863\t            })?;\n864\t            candidates.into_iter().map(|c| (beads_dir.parent().unwrap_or(beads_dir).to_path_buf(), c)).collect()\n865\t        };\n866\t\n867\t        if let Some((path, candidate)) = candidates.first() {\n868\t            match format {\n869\t                \"json\" => {\n870\t                    let output = serde_json::json!({\n871\t                        \"bead_id\": candidate.id,\n872\t                        \"title\": candidate.title,\n873\t                        \"priority\": candidate.priority,\n874\t                        \"downstream_impact\": candidate.downstream_impact,\n875\t                        \"assignee\": assignee,\n876\t                        \"workspace\": path.display().to_string(),\n877\t                        \"dry_run\": true\n878\t                    });\n879\t                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n880\t                }\n881\t                _ => {\n882\t                    println!(\"{} (priority={}, impact={}, workspace={})\",\n883\t                        candidate.id, candidate.priority, candidate.downstream_impact, path.display());\n884\t                }\n885\t            }\n886\t        } else if format == \"json\" {\n887\t            println!(\"{{}}\");\n888\t        } else {\n889\t            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n890\t        }\n891\t    } else if any {\n892\t        // Claim from any workspace\n893\t        let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n894\t            // Auto-discover workspaces from current directory\n895\t            find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n896\t        } else {\n897\t            workspace_paths.to_vec()\n898\t        };\n899\t\n900\t        let result = claim_any(&paths, assignee, claim_ttl, Some(&worker_metadata))?;\n901\t\n902\t        match result {\n903\t            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, workspace_path }) => {\n904\t                match format {\n905\t                    \"json\" => {\n906\t                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n907\t                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n908\t                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n909\t                            \"assignee\": assignee,\n910\t                            \"workspace\": workspace_path.map(|p| p.display().to_string())\n911\t                        });\n912\t                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n913\t                    }\n914\t                    _ => {\n915\t                        if let Some(path) = workspace_path {\n916\t                            println!(\"{} (workspace: {})\", bead_id, path.display());\n917\t                        } else {\n918\t                            println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n919\t                        }\n920\t                    }\n921\t                }\n922\t            }\n923\t            None => {\n924\t                if format == \"json\" {\n925\t                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n926\t                } else {\n927\t                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n928\t                }\n929\t            }\n930\t        }\n931\t    } else if fallback {\n932\t        // Fallback mode: try current workspace first, then any\n933\t        let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n934\t        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n935\t        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n936\t\n937\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n938\t            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now(), Some(&worker_metadata))\n939\t        })?;\n940\t\n941\t        match result {\n942\t            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, .. }) => {\n943\t                match format {\n944\t                    \"json\" => {\n945\t                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n946\t                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n947\t                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n948\t                            \"assignee\": assignee\n949\t                        });\n950\t                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n951\t                    }\n952\t                    _ => {\n953\t                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n954\t                    }\n955\t                }\n956\t            }\n957\t            None => {\n958\t                // Fallback to any workspace\n959\t                let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n960\t                    find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n961\t                } else {\n962\t                    workspace_paths.to_vec()\n963\t                };\n964\t\n965\t                let result = claim_any(&paths, assignee, claim_ttl, Some(&worker_metadata))?;\n966\t\n967\t                match result {\n968\t                    Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, workspace_path }) => {\n969\t                        match format {\n970\t                            \"json\" => {\n971\t                                let output = serde_json::json!({\n972\t                                    \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n973\t                                    \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n974\t                                    \"assignee\": assignee,\n975\t                                    \"workspace\": workspace_path.map(|p| p.display().to_string())\n976\t                                });\n977\t                                println!(\"{}\", output);\n978\t                            }\n979\t                            _ => {\n980\t                                if let Some(path)
