        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n984\t                                }\n985\t                            }\n986\t                        }\n987\t                    }\n988\t                    None => {\n989\t                        if format == \"json\" {\n990\t                            println!(\"{{}}\");\n991\t                        } else {\n992\t                            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n993\t                        }\n994\t                    }\n995\t                }\n996\t            }\n997\t        }\n998\t    } else {\n999\t        // Normal single-workspace claim\n1000\t        let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1001\t        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n1002\t        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n1003\t\n1004\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n1005\t            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now(), Some(&worker_metadata))\n1006\t        })?;\n1007\t\n1008\t        match result {\n1009\t            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, .. }) => {\n1010\t                match format {\n1011\t                    \"json\" => {\n1012\t                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n1013\t                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n1014\t                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n1015\t                            \"assignee\": assignee\n1016\t                        });\n1017\t                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n1018\t                    }\n1019\t                    _ => {\n1020\t                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n1021\t                    }\n1022\t                }\n1023\t            }\n1024\t            None => {\n1025\t                if format == \"json\" {\n1026\t                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n1027\t                } else {\n1028\t                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n1029\t                }\n1030\t            }\n1031\t        }\n1032\t    }\n1033\t\n1034\t    Ok(())\n1035\t}\n1036\t\n1037\tfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n1038\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1039\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n1040\t    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n1041\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n1042\t\n1043\t    if import_only {\n1044\t        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n1045\t        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n1046\t    } else if flush_only {\n1047\t        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n1048\t        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n1049\t    } else {\n1050\t        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n1051\t        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n1052\t    }\n1053\t\n1054\t    Ok(())\n1055\t}\n1056\t\n1057\tfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n1058\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1059\t    let db_path = beads\", count);\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    Ok(())\n1074\t}\n1075\t\n1076\tfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n1077\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1078\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n1079\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n1080\t\n1081\t    let count = if let Some(s) = status     storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n1087\t    } else {\n1088\t        storage.count_issues()?\n1089\t    };\n1090\t\n1091\t    println!(\"{}\", count);\n1092\t    Ok(())\n1093\t}\n1094\t\n1095\tfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n1096\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1097\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n1098\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n1099\t\n1100\t    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n1101\t        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n1102\t    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n1103\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n1104\t        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n1105\t    } else if stdin {\n1106\t        parse_stdin()?\n1107\t    } else {\n1108\t        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n1109\t    };\n1110\t\n1111\t    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n1112\t\n1113\t    // Print results\n1114\t    for result in results {\n1115\t        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n1116\t            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n1117\t                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n1118\t            } else {\n1119\t                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n1120\t            }\n1121\t        } else {\n1122\t            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n1123\t        }\n1124\t    }\n1125\t\n1126\t    Ok(())\n1127\t}\n1128\t\n1129\tfn cmd_mitosis(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, children: &str, reason: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n1130\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n1131\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n1132\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n1133\t\n1134\t    // Parse children as JSON array of {title, type, priority} or {title, type, priority, description, assignee, labels}\n1135\t    let children_defs: Vec<MitosisChild> = serde_json::from_str(children)?;\n1136\t\n1137\t    // Build the batch operations\n1138\t    let ops = {\n1144\t        \"json\" => {\n1145\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&results)?);\n1146\t        }\n1147\t        _ => {\n1148\t            // Print child IDs that were created\n1149\t            for result in &results {\n1150\t                if let Some(child_id) = &result.id {\n1151\t                    println!(\"Created child: {}\", child_id);\n1152\t                }\n1153\t            }\n1154\t            println!(\"Parent bead {} closed with {} children\", id, results.len() - 2); // -2 for close + last dep\n1155\t        }\n1156\t    }\n1157\t\n1158\t    Ok(())\n1159\t}\n1160\t\n1161\tfn cmd_dep(beads_dir: &PathBuf, dep: DepCommands) -> Result<()> {\n1162\t    match dep {\n1163\t        DepCommands::Add { issue, depends_on, type_ } => {\n1164\t cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n\n    // For init command, we allow the .beads directory to not exist yet\n    match &cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { .. } => {\n            let beads_dir = workspace.join(\".beads\");\n            return match cli.command {\n                Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n                _ => workspace))?;\n\n    match cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n        Commands::Create { title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label } => {\n            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n        }\n        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Show { id, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n        }\n        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Ready { limit, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, any, fallback, workspace_paths, dry_run, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, any, fallback, &workspace_paths, dry_run, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n        Commands::Mitosis { id, children, reason, format } => cmd_mitosis(&beads_dir, &id, &children, &reason, &format),\n        Commands::Dep(dep) => cmd_dep(&beads_dir, dep),\n        Commands::Label(label) => cmd_label(&beads_dir, label),\n        Commands::Comments(comments) => cmd_comments(&beads_dir, comments),\n        Commands::Search { query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, format } => {\n            cmd_search(&beads_dir, query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Stats { by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, format } => {\n            cmd_stats(&beads_dir, by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Schema { target, format } => cmd_schema(&target, &format),\n        Commands::Config(config) => cmd_config(&beads_dir, config),\n        Commands::Velocity { model, harness, format } => cmd_velocity(&beads_dir, model, harness, &format),\n        Commands::Labels { id, format } => cmd_labels(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n    }\n}\n\nfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n\n    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n    if !config_path.exists() {\n        let config = format!(\n            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\ndefault_priority: 2\ndefault_type: task\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n\"#,\n            prefix\n        );\n        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n    }\n\n    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n    }\n\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_create(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    title: String,\n    type_: String,\n    priority: i32,\n    description: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    labels: Vec<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n\n    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n    issue.description = description;\n    issue.assignee = assignee;\n    issue.labels = labels;\n\n    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n\n    println!(\"{}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_list(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    type_: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    filter.assignee = assignee;\n    filter.priority = priority;\n    // --limit 0 means unlimited\n    filter.limit = limit.and_then(|l| if l == 0 { None } else { Some(l) });\n\n    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issues)?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            // Strip dependencies/comments before serializing: NEEDLE's BrDependency\n            // format ({id, title, status, priority, dependency_type}) differs from\n            // bead-forge's Dependency format ({issue_id, depends_on_id, type, ...}).\n            // NEEDLE has #[serde(default)] on the deps field so empty is fine.\n            let mut out = issue;\n            out.dependencies = vec![];\n            out.comments = vec![];\n            // Wrap in array so NEEDLE's parse_single_bead (Vec<Bead> → first) works.\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&vec![out])?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n            println!(\"Status: {}\", issue.status);\n            println!(\"Priority: {}\", issue.priority);\n            println!(\"Type: {}\", issue.issue_type);\n            if let Some(desc) = &issue.description {\n                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n            }\n            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n            }\n            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_update(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    id: &str,\n    title: Option<String>,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut changes = IssueChanges {\n        title,\n        status: status.map(|s| Status::from_str(&s).ok()).flatten(),\n        priority,\n        assignee,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Updated bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_close(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.close_issue(id, reason, \"cli\")?;\n    println!(\"Closed bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let changes = IssueChanges {\n        status: Some(Status::Open),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Reopened bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_delete(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    })?;\n\n    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        get_ready_candidates(tx, limit)\n    })?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for candidate in candidates {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&candidate)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for candidate in candidates {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} (priority={}, impact={})\",\n                    candidate.id, candidate.title, candidate.priority,\n                    candidate.downstream_impact);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_claim(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    assignee: &str,\n    model: Option<String>,\n    harness: Option<String>,\n    harness_version: Option<String>,\n    any: bool,\n    fallback: bool,\n    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n    dry_run: bool,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n\n    // Build worker metadata\n    let worker_metadata = WorkerMetadata {\n        worker_id: assignee.to_string(),\n        model: model.clone(),\n        harness: harness.clone(),\n        harness_version: harness_version.clone(),\n    };\n\n    if dry_run {\n        // Dry run mode - show what would be claimed\n        let candidates = if any || fallback {\n            // Multi-workspace dry run\n            let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n                // Auto-discover workspaces from current directory\n                find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n            } else {\n                workspace_paths.to_vec()\n            };\n\n            let mut all_candidates = Vec::new();\n            for path in &paths {\n                let local_beads_dir = path.join(\".beads\");\n                if local_beads_dir.exists() {\n                    let local_metadata = match load_metadata(&local_beads_dir) {\n                        Ok(m) => m,\n                        Err(_) => continue,\n                    };\n                    let local_db_path = local_beads_dir.join(&local_metadata.database);\n                    if let Ok(local_storage) = Storage::open(&local_db_path) {\n                        if let Ok(local_candidates) = local_storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n                            get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n                        }) {\n                            for c in local_candidates {\n                                all_candidates.push((path.clone(), c));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Sort by score and take top 1\n            all_candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                let score_a = (b.1.downstream_impact, b.1.priority, b.1.created_at.clone());\n                let score_b = (a.1.downstream_impact, a.1.priority, a.1.created_at.clone());\n                score_a.cmp(&score_b)\n            });\n            all_candidates.into_iter().take(1).collect()\n        } else {\n            // Single workspace dry run\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n                get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n            })?;\n            candidates.into_iter().map(|c| (beads_dir.parent().unwrap_or(beads_dir).to_path_buf(), c)).collect()\n        };\n\n        if let Some((path, candidate)) = candidates.first() {\n            match format {\n                \"json\" => {\n                    let output = serde_json::json!({\n                        \"bead_id\": candidate.id,\n                        \"title\": candidate.title,\n                        \"priority\": candidate.priority,\n                        \"downstream_impact\": candidate.downstream_impact,\n                        \"assignee\": assignee,\n                        \"workspace\": path.display().to_string(),\n                        \"dry_run\": true\n                    });\n                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    println!(\"{} (priority={}, impact={}, workspace={})\",\n                        candidate.id, candidate.priority, candidate.downstream_impact, path.display());\n                }\n            }\n        } else if format == \"json\" {\n            println!(\"{{}}\");\n        } else {\n            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n        }\n    } else if any {\n        // Claim from any workspace\n        let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n            // Auto-discover workspaces from current directory\n            find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n        } else {\n            workspace_paths.to_vec()\n        };\n\n        let result = claim_any(&paths, assignee, claim_ttl, Some(&worker_metadata))?;\n\n        match result {\n            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, workspace_path }) => {\n                match format {\n                    \"json\" => {\n                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                            \"assignee\": assignee,\n                            \"workspace\": workspace_path.map(|p| p.display().to_string())\n                        });\n                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        if let Some(path) = workspace_path {\n                            println!(\"{} (workspace: {})\", bead_id, path.display());\n                        } else {\n                            println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            None => {\n                if format == \"json\" {\n                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n                } else {\n                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if fallback {\n        // Fallback mode: try current workspace first, then any\n        let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now(), Some(&worker_metadata))\n        })?;\n\n        match result {\n            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, .. }) => {\n                match format {\n                    \"json\" => {\n                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                            \"assignee\": assignee\n                        });\n                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            None => {\n                // Fallback to any workspace\n                let paths = if workspace_paths.is_empty() {\n                    find_workspaces(&std::env::current_dir()?)?\n                } else {\n                    workspace_paths.to_vec()\n                };\n\n                let result = claim_any(&paths, assignee, claim_ttl, Some(&worker_metadata))?;\n\n                match result {\n                    Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, workspace_path }) => {\n                        match format {\n                            \"json\" => {\n                                let output = serde_json::json!({\n                                    \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                                    \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                                    \"assignee\": assignee,\n                                    \"workspace\": workspace_path.map(|p| p.display().to_string())\n                                });\n                                println!(\"{}\", output);\n                            }\n                            _ => {\n                                if let Some(path) = workspace_path {\n                                    println!(\"{} (workspace: {})\", bead_id, path.display());\n                                } else {\n                                    println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    None => {\n                        if format == \"json\" {\n                            println!(\"{{}}\");\n                        } else {\n                            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Normal single-workspace claim\n        let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now(), Some(&worker_metadata))\n        })?;\n\n        match result {\n            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed, .. }) => {\n                match format {\n                    \"json\" => {\n                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                            \"assignee\": assignee\n                        });\n                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            None => {\n                if format == \"json\" {\n                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n                } else {\n                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    if import_only {\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n    } else if flush_only {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    } else {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    if repair {\n        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n        let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n        let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Repaired database: imported {} beads from JSONL\", result.imported);\n    } else {\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n        println!(\"Database is healthy: {} beads\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = if let Some(s) = status {\n        let filter = IssueFilter {\n            status: Some(Status::from_str(&s).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n    } else {\n        storage.count_issues()?\n    };\n\n    println!(\"{}\", count);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n    } else if stdin {\n        parse_stdin()?\n    } else {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n    };\n\n    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n\n    // Print results\n    for result in results {\n        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n            } else {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n            }\n        } else {\n            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_mitosis(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, children: &str, reason: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    // Parse children as JSON array of {title, type, priority} or {title, type, priority, description, assignee, labels}\n    let children_defs: Vec<MitosisChild> = serde_json::from_str(children)?;\n\n    // Build the batch operations\n    let ops = mitosis_ex(id, children_defs, Some(reason.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Execute atomically\n    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&results)?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            // Print child IDs that were created\n            for result in &results {\n                if let Some(child_id) = &result.id {\n                    println!(\"Created child: {}\", child_id);\n                }\n            }\n            println!(\"Parent bead {} closed with {} children\", id, results.len() - 2); // -2 for close + last dep\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_dep(beads_dir: &PathBuf, dep: DepCommands) -> Result<()> {\n    match dep {\n        DepCommands::Add { issue, depends_on, type_ } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let dep_type = crate::model::DependencyType::from_str(&type_)\n                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n            storage.add_dependency(&issue, &depends_on, &dep_type, \"cli\")?;\n            println!(\"Added dependency: {} depends on {} ({})\", issue, depends_on, type_);\n        }\n        DepCommands::Remove { issue, depends_on } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            storage.remove_dependency(&issue, &depends_on)?;\n            println!(\"Removed dependency: {} -> {}\", issue, depends_on);\n        }\n        DepCommands::List { id } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let deps = storage.get_dependencies(&id)?;\n            if deps.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"No dependencies found for {}\", id);\n            } else {\n                for dep in deps {\n                    println!(\"  {} depends on {} ({})\", dep.issue_id, dep.depends_on_id, dep.dep_type);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        DepCommands::Tree { id, direction: _, max_depth: _ } => {\n            println!(\"Dependency tree for {}\", id);\n            println!(\"(tree view not yet implemented)\");\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_label(beads_dir: &PathBuf, label: LabelCommands) -> Result<()> {\n    match label {\n        LabelCommands::Add { id, label } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            for l in label {\n                storage.add_label(&id, &l)?;\n                println!(\"Added label '{}' to {}\", l, id);\n            }\n        }\n        LabelCommands::Remove { id, label } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            for l in label {\n                storage.remove_label(&id, &l)?;\n                println!(\"Removed label '{}' from {}\", l, id);\n            }\n        }\n        LabelCommands::List { id } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            if let Some(id) = id {\n                let labels = storage.get_labels(&id)?;\n                println!(\"Labels for {}:\", id);\n                for label in Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n    let labels = storage.get_labels(id)?;\n    if format == \"json\" {\n        println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&labels)?);\n    } else {\n        for label in CommentsCommands::Add { id, text } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let comment_text = text.join(\" \");\n            let comment_id = storage.add_comment(&id, \"cli\", &comment_text)?;\n            println!(\"Added comment {} to {}\", comment_id, id);\n        }\n        CommentsCommands::List { id } => {\n            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let comments = storage.list_comments(&id)?;\n            if comments.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"No comments for {}\", id);\n            } else {\n                for comment in comments {\n                    println!(\"  [{}] {}: {}\", comment.id, comment.author, comment.body);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_search(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    query: Option<String>,\n    status: Vec<String>,\n    type_: Vec<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    label: Vec<String>,\n    priority_min: Option<i32>,\n    priority_max: Option<i32>,\n    limit: usize,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let statuses: Vec<Status> = status.iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| Status::from_str(s).ok())\n        .collect();\n    let types: Vec<IssueType> = type_.iter()\n        .filter_map(|t| IssueType::from_str(t).ok())\n        .collect();\n\n    let issues = storage.search_issues(\n        query.as_deref(),\n        &statuses,\n        &types,\n        assignee.as_deref(),\n        &label,\n        priority_min,\n        priority_max,\n        limit,\n    )?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issue)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_stats(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    by_type: bool,\n    by_priority: bool,\n    by_assignee: bool,\n    by_label: bool,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path =      let labels = storage.list_all_labels()?;\n        println!(\"\\nBy label:\");\n        for (label, count) in labels {\n            println!(\"  {} ({})\", label, count);\n        }\n    }\n\n    }\n        _ => {\n            println!(\"Schema for {}: (not yet implemented)\", target);\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_config(beads_dir: &PathBuf, config: ConfigCommands) -> 'parent-child' OR type = 'conditional-blocks' OR type = 'waits-for');\n114\t\n115\t    -- Labels\n116\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS labels (\n117\t        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n118\t        label TEXT NOT NULL,\n119\t        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label),\n120\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n121\t    );\n122\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_label ON labels(label);\n123\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_issue ON labels(issue_id);\n124\t\n125\t    -- Comments\n126\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments (\n127\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n128\t        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n129\t        author TEXT NOT NULL,\n130\t        text TEXT NOT NULL,\n131\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n132\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n133\t    );\n134\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id);\n135\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_created_at ON comments(created_at);\n136\t\n137\t    -- Events (Audit)\n138\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (\n139\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n140\t        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n141\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n142\t        actor TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n143\t        old_value TEXT,\n144\t        new_value TEXT,\n145\t        comment TEXT,\n146\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n147\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n148\t    );\n149\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);\n150\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);\n151\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_created_at ON events(created_at);\n152\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n153\t\n154\t    -- Config (Runtime)\n155\t    -- NOTE: Avoid PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE constraints here because the current\n156\t    -- storage engine does not reliably maintain unique autoindexes.\n157\t    -- Application code enforces key replacement via DELETE + INSERT.\n158\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS config (\n159\t        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n160\t        value TEXT NOT NULL\n161\t    );\n162\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_config_key ON config(key);\n163\t\n164\t    -- Metadata\n165\t    -- Same rationale as config: keep it as key-value with explicit index.\n166\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metadata (\n167\t        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n168\t        value TEXT NOT NULL\n169\t    );\n170\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metadata_key ON metadata(key);\n171\t\n172\t    -- Dirty Issues (for export)\n173\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dirty_issues (\n174\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n175\t        marked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n176\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n177\t    );\n178\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dirty_issues_marked_at ON dirty_issues(marked_at);\n179\t\n180\t    -- Export Hashes (for incremental export)\n181\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS export_hashes (\n182\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n183\t        content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n184\t        exported_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n185\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n186\t    );\n187\t\n188\t    -- Blocked Issues Cache (Materialized view)\n189\t    -- Rebuilt on dependency or status changes.\n190\t    -- `blocked_by` stores a JSON array of blocking issue IDs.\n191\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n192\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n193\t        blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n194\t        blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n195\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n196\t    );\n197\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at);\n198\t\n199\t    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n200\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n201\t        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n202\t        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n203\t        FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n204\t    );\n205\t\n206\t    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n207\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n208\t        correlation_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n209\t        started_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n210\t        completed_at TEXT,\n211\t        final_outcome TEXT,\n212\t        session_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n213\t        trigger_reason TEXT,\n214\t        workspace_path idx_recovery_sessions_outcome ON recovery_sessions(final_outcome);\n219\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_type ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n220\t\n221\t    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n222\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n223\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n224\t        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n225\t        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n226\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n227\t        anomaly_type TEXT,\n228\t        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n229\t        outcome TEXT,\n230\t        evidence TEXT,\n231\t        metadata TEXT,\n232\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n233\t        FOREIGN KEY (correlation_id) REFERENCES EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n237\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n238\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n239\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n240\t\n241\t    -- Critical Path Cache (for impact-weighted claim scoring)\n242\t    -- Computed from dependency graph; lower float = more critical (0 = on critical path)\n243\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS critical_path_cache (\n244\t        bead_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n245\t        epic_id     TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),\n246\t        es          INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- earliest start (hops from root)\n247\t        ls          INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- latest start\n248\t        float       INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- ls - es; 0 = critical path\n249\t        updated_at  DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n250\t        FOREIGN KEY (bead_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n251\t    );\n252\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_cache_epic ON critical_path_cache(epic_id);\n253\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_cache_float ON critical_path_cache(float);\n254\t\n255\t    -- Bead Annotations (bf-only table, never touched by br)\n256\t    -- Stores arbitrary key-value metadata per bead.\n257\t    -- IMPORTANT: This is a SEPARATE table (not a column on issues) because br's\n258\t    -- issues_column_order_matches() check triggers rebuild_issues_table() when\n259\t    -- the column count differs, which would silently destroy any extra column.\n260\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_annotations (\n261\t        bead_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n262\t        key     TEXT NOT NULL,\n263\t        value   TEXT NOT NULL,\n264\t        PRIMARY KEY (bead_id, key)\n265\t    );\n266\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_annotations_key_value\n267\t        ON bead_annotations (key, value);\n268\t\n269\t    -- Worker Sessions (bf-only table for multi-workspace claiming)\n270\t    -- Tracks worker metadata (model, harness, version) for each claim operation.\n271\t    -- Used by velocity-aware scoring and audit trails.\n272\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS worker_sessions (\n273\t        worker_id        TEXT NOT NULL,\n274\t        model            TEXT,\n275\t        harness          TEXT,\n276\t        harness_version  TEXT,\n277\t        claimed_at       DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n278\t        bead_id          TEXT REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n279\t        workspace_path   TEXT NOT NULL,\n280\t        PRIMARY KEY (worker_id, claimed_at)\n281\t    );\n282\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_worker ON worker_sessions(worker_id);\n283\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_model ON worker_sessions(model);\n284\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_harness ON worker_sessions(harness);\n285\t\";\n286\t\n287\t/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n288\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n289\t///\n290\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n291\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n292\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n293\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n294\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n295\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n296\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n297\t///\n298\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n299\t/// statement terminators.\n300\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n301\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n302\t    let len = bytes.len();\n303\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n304\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n305\t    let mut i = 0;\n306\t\n307\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n308\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n309\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n310\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n311\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n312\t\n313\t    while i < len {\n314\t        let b = bytes[i];\n315\t\n316\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n317\t        if in_line_comment {\n318\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n319\t                in_line_comment = false;\n320\t            }\n321\t            i += 1;\n322\t            continue;\n323\t        }\n324\t\n325\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n326\t        if in_block_comment {\n327\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n328\t                in_block_comment = false;\n329\t                i += 2;\n330\t            } else {\n331\t                i += 1;\n332\t            }\n333\t            continue;\n334\t        }\n335\t\n336\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n337\t        if in_single_quote {\n338\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n339\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n340\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n341\t                    i += 2;\n342\t                } else {\n343\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n344\t                    i += 1;\n345\t                }\n346\t            } else {\n347\t                i += 1;\n348\t            }\n349\t            continue;\n350\t        }\n351\t\n352\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n353\t        if in_double_quote {\n354\t            if b == b'\"' {\n355\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n356\t                    i += 2;\n357\t                } else {\n358\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n359\t                    i += 1;\n360\t                }\n361\t            } else {\n362\t                i += 1;\n363\t            }\n364\t            continue;\n365\t        }\n366\t\n367\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n368\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n369\t            in_single_quote = true;\n370\t            i += 1;\n371\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n372\t            in_double_quote = true;\n373\t            i += 1;\n374\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n375\t            in_line_comment = true;\n376\t            i += 2;\n377\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n378\t            in_block_comment = true;\n379\t            i += 2;\n380\t        } else if b == b';' {\n381\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n382\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n383\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n384\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n385\t            }\n386\t            start = i + 1;\n387\t            i += 1;\n388\t        } else {\n389\t            i += 1;\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n394\t    if start < len {\n395\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n396\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n397\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n398\t        }\n399\t    }\n400\t\n401\t    stmts\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n405\tfn execute_batch(conn: &Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n406\t    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n407\t    Ok(())\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n411\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n412\t    Ok(())\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\tpub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n416\t    // Use execute_batch so PRAGMAs that return rows don't cause errors\n417\t    conn.execute_batch(\n418\t        \"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\n419\t         PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\n420\t         PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000;\n421\t         PRAGMA cache_size = -8000;\n422\t         PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;\"\n423\t    )?;\n424\t    Ok(())\n425\t}\n426\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"938d2fcc-9ce9-46ae-8d15-4e3bc7c79a7a","uuid":"fa9f73b3-f007-4583-813f-1a26fca15aad","timestamp":"2026-05-01T13:02:09.920Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/src/storage/schema.rs","content":"//! Database schema definitions matching beads_rust (br) for interoperability.\n\nuse rusqlite::Connection;\n\n/// The complete SQL schema for the beads database.\n/// Schema matches beads_rust (br) for interoperability.\npub const SCHEMA_SQL: &str = r\"\n    -- Issues table\n    -- Note: TEXT fields use DEFAULT '' for bd (Go) compatibility.\n    -- bd's sql.Scan doesn't handle NULL well when scanning into string fields.\n    -- Closed-at invariant is enforced by the CHECK clause below.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (\n        id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        content_hash TEXT,\n        title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500),\n        description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        design TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        acceptance_criteria TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        notes TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',\n        priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2 CHECK(priority >= 0 AND priority <= 4),\n        issue_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task',\n        assignee TEXT,\n        owner TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        estimated_minutes INTEGER,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        created_by TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        closed_at DATETIME,\n        close_reason TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        closed_by_session TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        due_at DATETIME,\n        defer_until DATETIME,\n        external_ref TEXT,\n        source_system TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        source_repo TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '.',\n        deleted_at DATETIME,\n        deleted_by TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        delete_reason TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        original_type TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        compaction_level INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n        compacted_at DATETIME,\n        compacted_at_commit TEXT,\n        original_size INTEGER,\n        sender TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        ephemeral INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        pinned INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        is_template INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        CHECK (\n            (status = 'closed' AND closed_at IS NOT NULL) OR\n            (status = 'tombstone') OR\n            (status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone') AND closed_at IS NULL)\n        )\n    );\n\n    -- Primary access patterns\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status ON issues(status);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_priority ON issues(priority);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_issue_type ON issues(issue_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_assignee ON issues(assignee) WHERE assignee IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_created_at ON issues(created_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_updated_at ON issues(updated_at);\n\n    -- Export/sync patterns\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_content_hash ON issues(content_hash);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref_unique ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n\n    -- Special states\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ephemeral ON issues(ephemeral) WHERE ephemeral = 1;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_pinned ON issues(pinned) WHERE pinned = 1;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_tombstone ON issues(status) WHERE status = 'tombstone';\n\n    -- Time-based\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_due_at ON issues(due_at) WHERE due_at IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_defer_until ON issues(defer_until) WHERE defer_until IS NOT NULL;\n\n    -- Ready work composite index (most important for performance)\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready\n        ON issues(status, priority, created_at)\n        WHERE status = 'open'\n        AND ephemeral = 0\n        AND pinned = 0\n        AND is_template = 0;\n\n    -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_list_active_order\n        ON issues(priority, created_at DESC)\n        WHERE status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')\n        AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL);\n\n    -- Dependencies\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        depends_on_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks',\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        created_by TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\n        thread_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, depends_on_id),\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n        -- Note: depends_on_id FK intentionally removed to allow external issue references\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_issue ON dependencies(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on ON dependencies(depends_on_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_type ON dependencies(type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on_type ON dependencies(depends_on_id, type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_thread ON dependencies(thread_id) WHERE thread_id != '';\n    -- Composite for blocking lookups\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_blocking\n        ON dependencies(depends_on_id, issue_id)\n        WHERE (type = 'blocks' OR type = 'parent-child' OR type = 'conditional-blocks' OR type = 'waits-for');\n\n    -- Labels\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS labels (\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        label TEXT NOT NULL,\n        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label),\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_label ON labels(label);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_issue ON labels(issue_id);\n\n    -- Comments\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        author TEXT NOT NULL,\n        text TEXT NOT NULL,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_created_at ON comments(created_at);\n\n    -- Events (Audit)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        actor TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        old_value TEXT,\n        new_value TEXT,\n        comment TEXT,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_created_at ON events(created_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n\n    -- Config (Runtime)\n    -- NOTE: Avoid PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE constraints here because the current\n    -- storage engine does not reliably maintain unique autoindexes.\n    -- Application code enforces key replacement via DELETE + INSERT.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS config (\n        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n        value TEXT NOT NULL\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_config_key ON config(key);\n\n    -- Metadata\n    -- Same rationale as config: keep it as key-value with explicit index.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metadata (\n        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n        value TEXT NOT NULL\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metadata_key ON metadata(key);\n\n    -- Dirty Issues (for export)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dirty_issues (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        marked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dirty_issues_marked_at ON dirty_issues(marked_at);\n\n    -- Export Hashes (for incremental export)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS export_hashes (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n        exported_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n\n    -- Blocked Issues Cache (Materialized view)\n    -- Rebuilt on dependency or status changes.\n    -- `blocked_by` stores a JSON array of blocking issue IDs.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n        blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at);\n\n    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n\n    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n        correlation_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        started_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n        completed_at TEXT,\n        final_outcome TEXT,\n        session_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        trigger_reason TEXT,\n        workspace_path TEXT,\n        summary TEXT\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_started ON recovery_sessions(started_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_outcome ON recovery_sessions(final_outcome);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_type ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n\n    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        anomaly_type TEXT,\n        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n        outcome TEXT,\n        evidence TEXT,\n        metadata TEXT,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n        FOREIGN KEY (correlation_id) REFERENCES recovery_sessions(correlation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_correlation ON anomaly_audit(correlation_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n\n    -- Critical Path Cache (for impact-weighted claim scoring)\n    -- Computed from dependency graph; lower float = more critical (0 = on critical path)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS critical_path_cache (\n        bead_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        epic_id     TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),\n        es          INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- earliest start (hops from root)\n        ls          INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- latest start\n        float       INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- ls - es; 0 = critical path\n        updated_at  DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (bead_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_cache_epic ON critical_path_cache(epic_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_cache_float ON critical_path_cache(float);\n\n    -- Bead Annotations (bf-only table, never touched by br)\n    -- Stores arbitrary key-value metadata per bead.\n    -- IMPORTANT: This is a SEPARATE table (not a column on issues) because br's\n    -- issues_column_order_matches() check triggers rebuild_issues_table() when\n    -- the column count differs, which would silently destroy any extra column.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_annotations (\n        bead_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n        key     TEXT NOT NULL,\n        value   TEXT NOT NULL,\n        PRIMARY KEY (bead_id, key)\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_annotations_key_value\n        ON bead_annotations (key, value);\n\n    -- Worker Sessions (bf-only table for multi-workspace claiming)\n    -- Tracks worker metadata (model, harness, version) for each claim operation.\n    -- Used by velocity-aware scoring and audit trails.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS worker_sessions (\n        worker_id        TEXT NOT NULL,\n        model            TEXT,\n        harness          TEXT,\n        harness_version  TEXT,\n        claimed_at       DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        bead_id          TEXT REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n        workspace_path   TEXT NOT NULL,\n        PRIMARY KEY (worker_id, claimed_at)\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_worker ON worker_sessions(worker_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_model ON worker_sessions(model);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_worker_sessions_harness ON worker_sessions(harness);\n\";\n\n/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n///\n/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n///\n/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n/// statement terminators.\nfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n    let len = bytes.len();\n    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n    let mut i = 0;\n\n    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n\n    while i < len {\n        let b = bytes[i];\n\n        // --- Line comment state ---\n        if in_line_comment {\n            if b == b'\\n' {\n                in_line_comment = false;\n            }\n            i += 1;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Block comment state ---\n        if in_block_comment {\n            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n                in_block_comment = false;\n                i += 2;\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n        if in_single_quote {\n            if b == b'\\'' {\n                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_single_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n        if in_double_quote {\n            if b == b'\"' {\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_double_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n        if b == b'\\'' {\n            in_single_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'\"' {\n            in_double_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n            in_line_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n            in_block_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b';' {\n            // Statement terminator at top level.\n            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n            }\n            start = i + 1;\n            i += 1;\n        } else {\n            i += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n    if start < len {\n        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n        }\n    }\n\n    stmts\n}\n\n/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\nfn execute_batch(conn: &Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn apply_schema(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    // Use execute_batch so PRAGMAs that return rows don't cause errors\n    conn.execute_batch(\n        \"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\n         PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\n         PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000;\n         PRAGMA cache_size = -8000;\n         PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;\"\n    )?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n","numLines":426,"startLine":1,"totalLines":426}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6f1b1457d8af40a984c18494","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\n4\tuse serde::Serialize;\n5\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n6\t\n7\t/// Worker metadata for tracking which model/harness claimed a bead\n8\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n9\tpub struct WorkerMetadata {\n10\t    pub worker_id: String,\n11\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n12\t    pub harness: Option<String>,\n13\t    pub harness_version: Option<String>,\n14\t}\n15\t\n16\t/// Result of a claim operation\n17\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n18\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n19\t    pub bead_id: String,\n20\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n21\t    pub workspace_path: Option<PathBuf>,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t/// Score for cross-workspace candidate comparison.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Higher scores are better. Ordered by:\n27\t/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\n28\t/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\n29\t/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\n30\t/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\n31\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\n32\tpub struct Score {\n33\t    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n34\t    pub critical_float: i64,\n35\t    pub priority: i32,\n36\t    pub created_at_ts: i64,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\timpl Score {\n40\t    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\n41\t    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\n42\t        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\n43\t    }\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\t/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n47\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n48\tpub struct ScoredBead {\n49\t    pub id: String,\n50\t    pub title: String,\n51\t    pub status: String,\n52\t    pub priority: i32,\n53\t    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n54\t    pub critical_float: f64,\n55\t    pub created_at: String,\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n59\t///\n60\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n61\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n62\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n63\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n64\t/// 4. Insert an event\n65\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n66\t/// 6. Commit\n67\t///\n68\t/// # Arguments\n69\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n70\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n71\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n72\t/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n73\t///\n74\t/// # Returns\n75\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n76\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n77\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n78\tpub fn claim(\n79\t    tx: &Connection,\n80\t    worker: &str,\n81\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n82\t    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n83\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n84\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n85\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n86\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n87\t        \"UPDATE issues\n88\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n89\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n90\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n91\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n92\t    )?;\n93\t\n94\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n95\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n96\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n97\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n98\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n99\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n100\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n101\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n102\t                i.priority\n103\t         FROM issues i\n104\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n105\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n106\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n107\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n108\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n109\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n110\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n111\t         GROUP BY i.id\n112\t         ORDER BY\n113\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n114\t             critical_float ASC,\n115\t             i.priority ASC,\n116\t             i.created_at ASC\n117\t         LIMIT 1\",\n118\t    )?;\n119\t\n120\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n121\t\n122\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n123\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n124\t\n125\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race    SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n130\t             WHERE id = ?       if rows_affected == 0 {\n136\t            return Ok(None);\n137\t        }\n138\t\n139\t        // Step 4: Insert event\n140\t        tx.execute(\n141\t            \"INSERT INTO events )?;\n145\t\n146\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n147\t        tx.execute(\n148\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n149\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n150\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n151\t        )?;\n152\t\n153\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n154\t            bead_id,\n155\t            reclaimed,\n156\t        }))\n157\t    } else load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n271\t            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n272\t            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n273\t\n274\t            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n275\t                claim(&tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, now)\n276\t            })? {\n277\t                Some(result) => Ok(Some((result, workspace_path.clone()))),\n278\t                None => Ok(None),\n279\t            }\n280\t        }\n281\t    }\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n285\t///\n286\t/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n287\t/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\n288\tfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf> {\n289\t    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n290\t    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n291\t        Ok(beads_dir)\n292\t    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\".beads\") {\n293\t        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\n294\t    } else {\n295\t        use anyhow::bail;\n296\t        bail!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace_path)\n297\t    }\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t#[cfg(test)]\n301\tmod tests {\n302\t    use super::*;\n303\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n304\t    use crate::model::{Issue,   #[test]\n349\t    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n350\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n351\t\n352\t        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n353\t        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n354\t        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n355\t        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n356\t        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n357\t        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n358\t\n359\t        // Create an open
