with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n364\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n365\t            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n366\t        }).unwrap();\n367\t\n368\t        assert!(result.is_some());\n369\t        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n370\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n371\t\n372\t        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n373\t        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n374\t        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n375\t    }\n376\t}\n377\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"938d2fcc-9ce9-46ae-8d15-4e3bc7c79a7a","uuid":"777c1524-cbb1-48e4-bffe-41e3ddc4c7bc","timestamp":"2026-05-01T13:03:06.736Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/src/claim.rs","content":"use anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\n/// Worker metadata for tracking which model/harness claimed a bead\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct WorkerMetadata {\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    pub model: Option<String>,\n    pub harness: Option<String>,\n    pub harness_version: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n    pub workspace_path: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\n/// Score for cross-workspace candidate comparison.\n///\n/// Higher scores are better. Ordered by:\n/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\n/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\n/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub struct Score {\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: i64,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub created_at_ts: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Score {\n    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\n    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\n        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct ScoredBead {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: f64,\n    pub created_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Connection,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race condition check\n        // The WHERE status = 'open' condition ensures we only claim if still open\n        let rows_affected = tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // If no rows were affected, another worker claimed this bead first\n        if rows_affected == 0 {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n///\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.created_at\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT ?1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            title: row.get(1)?,\n            status: row.get(2)?,\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n        });\n    }\n\n    Ok(candidates)\n}\n\n/// Claim from the highest-priority bead across multiple workspaces.\n///\n/// Scores each workspace's top candidate, picks the global winner,\n/// and claims from that workspace.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result, workspace_path))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim_any(\n    workspace_paths: &[std::path::PathBuf],\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<(ClaimResult, std::path::PathBuf)>> {\n    use crate::config::load_metadata;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n\n    // Score across all workspaces\n    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\n    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\n        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n        // Open each workspace's SQLite\n        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\n            Ok(storage) => {\n                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\n                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n                        best = Some((score, idx));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\n                continue;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    match best {\n        None => Ok(None),\n        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\n            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\n            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n                claim(&tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, now)\n            })? {\n                Some(result) => Ok(Some((result, workspace_path.clone()))),\n                None => Ok(None),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n///\n/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\nfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf> {\n    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n        Ok(beads_dir)\n    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\".beads\") {\n        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\n    } else {\n        use anyhow::bail;\n        bail!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace_path)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n and claims from that workspace.\n225\t///\n226\t/// # Arguments\n227\t/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\n228\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n229\t/// * Returns\n233\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed (with workspace_path set)\n234\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\n235\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n236\tpub fn claim_any(\n237\t    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n238\t    worker: &str,\n239\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n240\t    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n241\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n242\t    use crate::config::load_metadata;\n243\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n244\t\n245\t    // Score across all workspaces\n246\t    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\n247\t    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\n248\t        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n249\t        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n250\t        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n251\t\n252\t        // Open each workspace's SQLite\n253\t        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\n254\t            Ok(storage) => {\n255\t                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\n256\t                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n257\t                        best = Some((score, idx));\n258\t                    }\n259\t                }\n260\t            }\n261\t            Err(_) => {\n262\t                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\n263\t                continue;\n264\t            }\n265\t        }\n266\t    }\n267\t\n268\t    match best {\n269\t        None => Ok(None),\n270\t        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\n271\t            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\n272\t            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n273\t            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n274\t            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n275\t            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n276\t\n277\t            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n278\t                claim(tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, worker_metadata)\n279\t            })? {\n280\t                Some(mut result) => {\n281\t                    result.workspace_path = Some(workspace_path.clone());\n282\t                    Ok(Some(result))\n283\t                }\n284\t                None => Ok(None),\n285\t            }\n286\t        }\n287\t    }\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\t/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n291\t///\n292\t/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n293\t/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\n294\tfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf> {\n295\t    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n296\t    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n297\t        Ok(beads_dir)\n298\t    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\".beads\") {\n299\t        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\n300\t    } else {\n301\t        use anyhow::bail;\n302\t        bail!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace_path)\n303\t    }\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t#[cfg(test)]\n307\tmod tests {\n308\t    use super::*;\n309\t    use anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\n/// Worker metadata for tracking which model/harness claimed a bead\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct WorkerMetadata {\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    pub model: Option<String>,\n    pub harness: Option<String>,\n    pub harness_version: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n    pub workspace_path: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\n/// Score for cross-workspace candidate comparison.\n///\n/// Higher scores are better. Ordered by:\n/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\n/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\n/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub struct Score {\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: i64,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub created_at_ts: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Score {\n    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\n    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\n        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct ScoredBead {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: f64,\n    pub created_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Connection,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    let now = Utc::now();\n\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race condition check\n        // The WHERE status = 'open' condition ensures we only claim if still open\n        let rows_affected = tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // If no rows were affected, another worker claimed this bead first\n        if rows_affected == 0 {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n            workspace_path: None,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n///\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.created_at\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT ?1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            title: row.get(1)?,\n            status: row.get(2)?,\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n        });\n    }\n\n    Ok(candidates)\n}\n\n/// Claim from the highest-priority bead across multiple workspaces.\n///\n/// Scores each workspace's top candidate, picks the global winner,\n/// and claims from that workspace.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed (with workspace_path set)\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim_any(\n    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    use crate::config::load_metadata;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n\n    // Score across all workspaces\n    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\n    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\n        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n        // Open each workspace's SQLite\n        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\n            Ok(storage) => {\n                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\n                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n                        best = Some((score, idx));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\n                continue;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    match best {\n        None => Ok(None),\n        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\n            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\n            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n                claim(tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, worker_metadata)\n            })? {\n                Some(mut result) => {\n                    result.workspace_path = Some(workspace_path.clone());\n                    Ok(Some(result))\n                }\n                None => Ok(None),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n///\n/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\nfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf> {\n    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n        Ok(beads_dir)\n    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\".beads\") {\n        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\n    } else {\n        use anyhow::bail;\n        bail!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace_path)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","numLines":383,"startLine":1,"totalLines":383}}}

Ordered by:\\n/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\\n/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\\n/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\\n/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\\npub struct Score {\\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\\n    pub critical_float: i64,\\n    pub priority: i32,\\n    pub created_at_ts: i64,\\n}\\n\\nimpl Score {\\n    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\\n    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\\n        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\\npub struct ScoredBead {\\n    pub id: String,\\n    pub title: String,\\n    pub status: String,\\n    pub priority: i32,\\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\\n    pub critical_float: f64,\\n    pub created_at: String,\\n}\\n\\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\\n///\\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\\n/// 4. Record worker session if metadata provided\\n/// 5. Insert an event\\n/// 6. Mark the bead as dirty\\n/// 7. Commit\\n///\\n/// # Arguments\\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\\n///\\n/// # Returns\\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\\npub fn claim(\\n    tx: &Connection,\\n    worker: &str,\\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\\n        \\\"UPDATE issues\\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\\n           AND updated_at < ?\\\",\\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\\n    )?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\\n        \\\"SELECT i.id,\\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\\n                i.priority\\n         FROM issues i\\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\\n           AND i.pinned = 0\\n           AND i.is_template = 0\\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\\n         GROUP BY i.id\\n         ORDER BY\\n             downstream_impact DESC,\\n             critical_float ASC,\\n             i.priority ASC,\\n             i.created_at ASC\\n         LIMIT 1\\\",\\n    )?;\\n\\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\\n\\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\\n\\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race condition check\\n        // The WHERE status = 'open' condition ensures we only claim if still open\\n        let rows_affected = tx.execute(\\n            \\\"UPDATE issues\\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\\n             WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\\\",\\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\\n        )?;\\n\\n        // If no rows were affected, another worker claimed this bead first\\n        if rows_affected == 0 {\\n            return Ok(None);\\n        }\\n\\n        // Step 4: Record worker session if metadata provided\\n        if let Some(meta) = worker_metadata {\\n            tx.execute(\\n                \\\"INSERT INTO worker_sessions (worker_id, model, harness, harness_version, bead_id, workspace_path)\\n                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)\\\",\\n                params![\\n                    &meta.worker_id,\\n                    meta.model.as_deref(),\\n                    meta.harness.as_deref(),\\n                    meta.harness_version.as_deref(),\\n                    &bead_id,\\n                    \\\"\\\", // workspace_path not available in transaction context\\n                ],\\n            )?;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Step 5: Insert event\\n        tx.execute(\\n            \\\"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\\\",\\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\\n        )?;\\n\\n        // Step 6: Mark as dirty\\n        tx.execute(\\n            \\\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\\n             VALUES (?, ?)\\\",\\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\\n        )?;\\n\\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\\n            bead_id,\\n            reclaimed,\\n            workspace_path: None,\\n        }))\\n    } else {\\n        Ok(None)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\\n///\\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\\n///\\n/// # Arguments\\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\\n///\\n/// # Returns\\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\\n        \\\"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\\n                i.created_at\\n         FROM issues i\\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\\n           AND i.pinned = 0\\n           AND i.is_template = 0\\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\\n         GROUP BY i.id\\n         ORDER BY\\n             downstream_impact DESC,\\n             critical_float ASC,\\n             i.priority ASC,\\n             i.created_at ASC\\n         LIMIT ?1\\\",\\n    )?;\\n\\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\\n\\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\\n            id: row.get(0)?,\\n            title: row.get(1)?,\\n            status: row.get(2)?,\\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\\n        });\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(candidates)\\n}\\n\\n/// Claim from the highest-priority bead across multiple workspaces.\\n///\\n/// Scores each workspace's top candidate, picks the global winner,\\n/// and claims from that workspace.\\n///\\n/// # Arguments\\n/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\\n///\\n/// # Returns\\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed (with workspace_path set)\\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\\npub fn claim_any(\\n    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\\n    worker: &str,\\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\\n    use crate::config::load_metadata;\\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\\n\\n    // Score across all workspaces\\n    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\\n    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\\n        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\\n        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\\n\\n        // Open each workspace's SQLite\\n        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\\n            Ok(storage) => {\\n                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\\n                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\\n                        best = Some((score, idx));\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n            Err(_) => {\\n                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\\n                continue;\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    match best {\\n        None => Ok(None),\\n        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\\n            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\\n            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\\n            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\\n            let now = Utc::now();\\n\\n            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\\n                claim(tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, now, worker_metadata)\\n            })? {\\n                Some(mut result) => {\\n                    result.workspace_path = Some(workspace_path.clone());\\n                    Ok(Some(result))\\n                }\\n                None => Ok(None),\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Find all bead-forge workspaces under a given root path.\\n///\\n/// Searches for directories containing a `.beads/` subdirectory with a `beads.db` file.\\n/// Used by `bf claim --any` to discover all available workspaces.\\n///\\n/// # Arguments\\n/// * `root_path` - The root directory to start searching from\\n///\\n/// # Returns\\n/// * `Ok(Vec<PathBuf>)` - List of workspace directory paths\\npub fn find_workspaces(root_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>> {\\n    let mut workspaces = Vec::new();\\n\\n    // Check if root_path itself is a workspace\\n    let beads_dir = root_path.join(\\\".beads\\\");\\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(\\\"beads.db\\\");\\n        if db_path.is_file() {\\n            workspaces.push(root_path.to_path_buf());\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Search subdirectories for workspaces (up to 3 levels deep)\\n    let max_depth = 3;\\n    search_workspaces_recursive(root_path, &mut workspaces, 0, max_depth)?;\\n\\n    Ok(workspaces)\\n}\\n\\n/// Recursively search for workspaces.\\n///\\n/// Helper function for `find_workspaces()` that limits search depth.\\nfn search_workspaces_recursive(\\n    path: &Path,\\n    workspaces: &mut Vec<PathBuf>,\\n    current_depth: usize,\\n    max_depth: usize,\\n) -> Result<()> {\\n    if current_depth >= max_depth {\\n        return Ok(());\\n    }\\n\\n    let entries = match fs::read_dir(path) {\\n        Ok(e) => e,\\n        Err(_) => return Ok(()),\\n    };\\n\\n    for entry in entries.flatten() {\\n        let entry_path = entry.path();\\n        if entry_path.is_dir() {\\n            // Check if this is a workspace\\n            let beads_dir = entry_path.join(\\\".beads\\\");\\n            if beads_dir.is_dir() {\\n                let db_path = beads_dir.join(\\\"beads.db\\\");\\n                if db_path.is_file() {\\n                    workspaces.push(entry_path.clone());\\n                    continue; // Don't descend into workspace directories\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            // Recursively search subdirectories\\n            search_workspaces_recursive(&entry_path, workspaces, current_depth + 1, max_depth)?;\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\\n///\\n/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\\n/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\\nfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf> {\\n    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\\\".beads\\\");\\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\\n        Ok(beads_dir)\\n    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\\\".beads\\\") {\\n        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\\n    } else {\\n        anyhow::bail!(\\\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\\\", workspace_path)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use super::*;\\n tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\\n        (temp_file, storage)\\n    }\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\\n Ordered by:\n/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\n/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\n/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub struct Score {\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: i64,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub created_at_ts: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Score {\n    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\n    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\n        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct ScoredBead {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: f64,\n    pub created_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Record worker session if metadata provided\n/// 5. Insert an event\n/// 6. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 7. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Connection,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race condition check\n        // The WHERE status = 'open' condition ensures we only claim if still open\n        let rows_affected = tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // If no rows were affected, another worker claimed this bead first\n        if rows_affected == 0 {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Record worker session if metadata provided\n        if let Some(meta) = worker_metadata {\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO worker_sessions (worker_id, model, harness, harness_version, bead_id, workspace_path)\n                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)\",\n                params![\n                    &meta.worker_id,\n                    meta.model.as_deref(),\n                    meta.harness.as_deref(),\n                    meta.harness_version.as_deref(),\n                    &bead_id,\n                    \"\", // workspace_path not available in transaction context\n                ],\n            )?;\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 6: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n            workspace_path: None,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n///\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.created_at\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT ?1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            title: row.get(1)?,\n            status: row.get(2)?,\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n        });\n    }\n\n    Ok(candidates)\n}\n\n/// Claim from the highest-priority bead across multiple workspaces.\n///\n/// Scores each workspace's top candidate, picks the global winner,\n/// and claims from that workspace.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed (with workspace_path set)\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim_any(\n    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    use crate::config::load_metadata;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n\n    // Score across all workspaces\n    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\n    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\n        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n        // Open each workspace's SQLite\n        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\n            Ok(storage) => {\n                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\n                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n                        best = Some((score, idx));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\n                continue;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    match best {\n        None => Ok(None),\n        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\n            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\n            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n            let now = Utc::now();\n\n            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n                claim(tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, now, worker_metadata)\n            })? {\n                Some(mut result) => {\n                    result.workspace_path = Some(workspace_path.clone());\n                    Ok(Some(result))\n                }\n                None => Ok(None),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Find all bead-forge workspaces under a given root path.\n///\n/// Searches for directories containing a `.beads/` subdirectory with a `beads.db` file.\n/// Used by `bf claim --any` to discover all available workspaces.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `root_path` - The root directory to start searching from\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<PathBuf>)` - List of workspace directory paths\npub fn find_workspaces(root_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>> {\n    let mut workspaces = Vec::new();\n\n    // Check if root_path itself is a workspace\n    let beads_dir = root_path.join(\".beads\");\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n        if db_path.is_file() {\n            workspaces.push(root_path.to_path_buf());\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Search subdirectories for workspaces (up to 3 levels deep)\n    let max_depth = 3;\n    search_workspaces_recursive(root_path, &mut workspaces, 0, max_depth)?;\n\n    Ok(workspaces)\n}\n\n/// Recursively search for workspaces.\n///\n/// Helper function for `find_workspaces()` that limits search depth.\nfn search_workspaces_recursive(\n    path: &Path,\n    workspaces: &mut Vec<PathBuf>,\n    current_depth: usize,\n    max_depth: usize,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    if current_depth >= max_depth {\n        return Ok(());\n    }\n\n    let entries = match fs::read_dir(path) {\n        Ok(e) => e,\n        Err(_) => return Ok(()),\n    };\n\n    for entry in entries.flatten() {\n        let entry_path = entry.path();\n        if entry_path.is_dir() {\n            // Check if this is a workspace\n            let beads_dir = entry_path.join(\".beads\");\n            if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n                let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n                if db_path.is_file() {\n                    workspaces.push(entry_path.clone());\n                    continue; // Don't descend into workspace directories\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Recursively search subdirectories\n            search_workspaces_recursive(&entry_path, workspaces, current_depth + 1, max_depth)?;\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n///\n/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n/// Otherwise, assume the path IS the .beads directory.\nfn get_beads_dir(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf> {\n    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n    if beads_dir.is_dir() {\n        Ok(beads_dir)\n    } else if workspace_path.ends_with(\".beads\") {\n        Ok(workspace_path.to_path_buf())\n    } else {\n        anyhow::bail!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace_path)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now(), None)\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_with_worker_metadata() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test2\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with worker metadata\n        let metadata = WorkerMetadata {\n            worker_id: \"worker1\".to_string(),\n            model: Some(\"claude-sonnet-4-6\".to_string()),\n            harness: Some(\"needle\".to_string()),\n            harness_version: Some(\"0.5.2\".to_string()),\n        };\n\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now(), Some(&metadata))\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test2\");\n\n        // Verify worker session was recorded\n        let conn = storage.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let session_count: i64 = conn.query_row(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM worker_sessions WHERE worker_id = ?1\",\n            params![\"worker1\"],\n            |row| row.get(0)\n        ).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(session_count, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now(), None)\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now(), None)\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b02f8253a23a4a91bc7da85f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\n4\tuse serde::Serialize;\n5\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n6\t\n7\t/// Worker metadata for tracking which model/harness claimed a bead\n8\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n9\tpub struct WorkerMetadata {\n10\t    pub worker_id: String,\n11\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n12\t    pub harness: Option<String>,\n13\t    pub harness_version: Option<String>,\n14\t}\n15\t\n16\t/// Result of a claim operation\n17\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n18\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n19\t    pub bead_id: String,\n20\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n21\t    pub workspace_path: Option<PathBuf>,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t/// Score for cross-workspace candidate comparison.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Higher scores are better. Ordered by:\n27\t/// 1. downstream_impact (more blocking = higher priority)\n28\t/// 2. negative critical_float (lower float = more critical)\n29\t/// 3. negative priority (lower number = higher priority)\n30\t/// 4. negative created timestamp (older = higher priority/FIFO)\n31\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]\n32\tpub struct Score {\n33\t    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n34\t    pub critical_float: i64,\n35\t    pub priority: i32,\n36\t    pub created_at_ts: i64,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\timpl Score {\n40\t    /// Create a new score from candidate fields.\n41\t    pub fn new(downstream_impact: i64, critical_float: i64, priority: i32, created_at_ts: i64) -> Self {\n42\t        Self { downstream_impact, critical_float, priority, created_at_ts }\n43\t    }\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\t/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n47\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n48\tpub struct ScoredBead {\n49\t    pub id: String,\n50\t    pub title: String,\n51\t    pub status: String,\n52\t    pub priority: i32,\n53\t    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n54\t    pub critical_float: f64,\n55\t    pub created_at: String,\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n59\t///\n60\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n61\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n62\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n63\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n64\t/// 4. Insert an event\n65\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n66\t/// 6. Commit\n67\t///\n68\t/// # Arguments\n69\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n70\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n71\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n72\t/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n73\t///\n74\t/// # Returns\n75\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n76\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n77\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n78\tpub fn claim(\n79\t    tx: &Connection,\n80\t    worker: &str,\n81\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n82\t    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n83\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n84\t    let now = Utc::now();\n85\t\n86\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n87\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n88\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n89\t        \"UPDATE issues\n90\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n91\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n92\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n93\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n94\t    )?;\n95\t\n96\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n97\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n98\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n99\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n100\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n101\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n102\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n103\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n104\t                i.priority\n105\t         FROM issues i\n106\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n107\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n108\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n109\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n110\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n111\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n112\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n113\t         GROUP BY i.id\n114\t         ORDER BY\n115\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n116\t             critical_float ASC,\n117\t             i.priority ASC,\n118\t             i.created_at ASC\n119\t         LIMIT 1\",\n120\t    )?;\n121\t\n122\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n123\t\n124\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n125\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n126\t\n127\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress with a race condition check\n128\t        // The WHERE status = 'open' condition ensures we only claim if still open\n129\t        let rows_affected = tx.execute(\n130\t            \"UPDATE issues\n131\t             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n132\t             WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\",\n133\t            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n134\t        )?;\n135\t\n136\t        // If no rows were affected, another worker claimed this bead first\n137\t        if rows_affected == 0 {\n138\t            return Ok(None);\n139\t        }\n140\t\n141\t        // Step 4: Insert event with worker metadata in comment field\n142\t        let metadata_json = worker_metadata.and_then(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).ok());\n143\t        tx.execute(\n144\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, comment, created_at)\n145\t             VALUES (?, 'claimed', ?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n146\t            params![&bead_id, worker, worker, metadata_json, now.to_rfc3339()],\n147\t        )?;\n148\t\n149\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n150\t        tx.execute(\n151\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n152\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n153\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n154\t        )?;\n155\t\n156\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n157\t            bead_id,\n158\t            reclaimed,\n159\t            workspace_path: None,\n160\t        }))\n161\t    } else {\n162\t        Ok(None)\n163\t    }\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n167\t///\n168\t/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n169\t/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n170\t/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n171\t/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n172\t/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n173\t/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n174\t///\n175\t/// # Arguments\n176\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n177\t/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n178\t///\n179\t/// # Returns\n180\t/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\n181\tpub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n182\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n183\t        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n184\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n185\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n186\t                i.created_at\n187\t         FROM issues i\n188\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n189\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n190\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n191\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n192\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n193\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n194\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n195\t         GROUP BY i.id\n196\t         ORDER BY\n197\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n198\t             critical_float ASC,\n199\t             i.priority ASC,\n200\t             i.created_at ASC\n201\t         LIMIT ?1\",\n202\t    )?;\n203\t\n204\t    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n205\t    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n206\t\n207\t    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n208\t        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n209\t            id: row.get(0)?,\n210\t            title: row.get(1)?,\n211\t            status: row.get(2)?,\n212\t            priority: row.get(3)?,\n213\t            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n214\t            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n215\t            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n216\t        });\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    Ok(candidates)\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\t/// Claim from the highest-priority bead across multiple workspaces.\n223\t///\n224\t/// Scores each workspace's top candidate, picks the global winner,\n225\t/// and claims from that workspace.\n226\t///\n227\t/// # Arguments\n228\t/// * `workspace_paths` - Slice of workspace directory paths\n229\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n230\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n231\t/// * `worker_metadata` - Optional worker metadata (model, harness, version)\n232\t///\n233\t/// # Returns\n234\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed (with workspace_path set)\n235\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim in any workspace\n236\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n237\tpub fn claim_any(\n238\t    workspace_paths: &[PathBuf],\n239\t    worker: &str,\n240\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n241\t    worker_metadata: Option<&WorkerMetadata>,\n242\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n243\t    use crate::config::load_metadata;\n244\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n245\t\n246\t    // Score across all workspaces\n247\t    let mut best: Option<(Score, usize)> = None;\n248\t    for (idx, workspace_path) in workspace_paths.iter().enumerate() {\n249\t        let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n250\t        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n251\t        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n252\t\n253\t        // Open each workspace's SQLite\n254\t        match Storage::open(&db_path) {\n255\t            Ok(storage) => {\n256\t                if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score()? {\n257\t                    if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n258\t                        best = Some((score, idx));\n259\t                    }\n260\t                }\n261\t            }\n262\t            Err(_) => {\n263\t                // Skip workspaces that can't be opened (e.g., no .beads directory)\n264\t                continue;\n265\t            }\n266\t        }\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    match best {\n270\t        None => Ok(None),\n271\t        Some((_, workspace_idx)) => {\n272\t            let workspace_path = &workspace_paths[workspace_idx];\n273\t            let beads_dir = get_beads_dir(workspace_path)?;\n274\t            let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n275\t            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n276\t            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n277\t\n278\t            match storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n279\t                claim(tx, worker, claim_ttl_minutes, worker_metadata)\n280\t            })? {\n281\t                Some(mut result) => {\n282\t                    result.workspace_path = Some(workspace_path.clone());\n283\t                    Ok(Some(result))\n284\t                }\n285\t                None => Ok(None),\n286\t            }\n287\t        }\n288\t    }\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t/// Get the .beads directory from a workspace path.\n292\t///\n293\t/// If the workspace path itself contains a .beads directory, use it.\n294\t/// Otherwise,
